programme.desc: An family farm in the mid-west contains houses, barns, outbuildings, trailers, grain silos and buses full of old stuff and perhaps the biggest stash of undiscovered old motorcycles in the country. The collection was put together by their unnamed father. Seller agrees to have Dale broker rare Excelsior for high-end client. Dale has the buyer, but will he outbid his own client for the exclusive find?

programme.title: Helping Hands Garage - Clark Family Corvette
programme.desc: After the sudden passing of the family’s father, Amanda knew she had to raise 5 children on her own, but she also knew she couldn’t sell the family Corvette. Her husband, Mark, was a trained mechanic who loved working on the Corvette with his sons. Because they were so close to the finish line on the car they reached out to see if Helping Hands could complete the project. With the love and support from friends and family, the Helping Hands crew rebuilds the Corvette's 350 motor to give this family a keepsake from their father that they can remember forever.

programme.title: Helping Hands Garage - Everyday Heroes
programme.desc: Even everyday heroes need a Helping Hand. Cyndy has been a nurse for over 20 years and Bill served in the Air Force in the 90’s and then donned the badge, protecting and serving as a police officer. So when Cyndy got her dream Dodge Challenger and didn’t know how personalize it, the Helping Hands teams was ready to step in and help.

programme.title: Helping Hands Garage - Stored Camaro
programme.desc: After years of sitting in storage, the Wilburn family is determined to get their beloved 81’ Camaro back on the road. Katie and Collete along with the Helping Hands provide a little help and a lot of determination to help this Military family bring the Camaro back to life.

programme.title: Helping Hands Garage - Next Generation
programme.desc: When the Haddox family inherited a Chevelle they wanted to convert it from an old race car to a weekend driver. They don’t know where to start and their daughters want to learn how to work on the car, so they bring it into the Helping Hands Garage and we’ll see if the next generation can catch the car bug.

programme.title: Helping Hands Garage - Courageous Jeep
programme.desc: The Heinze family's courageous young daughter has been in and out of hospitals fighting a rare condition since she was born. There is no time for the family to work on their cherished Jeep or take any off-road trips. With the support of the Helping Hands team, their ride is revamped into something that brings them joy and adventure.

programme.title: Helping Hands Garage - Firefighter Mustang Surprise
programme.desc: A Mom wants to fix her firefighter son's dream Mustang before his upcoming wedding. The car has been dormant for years and Katie and Collete will put together the best Helping Hands team to give this Mustang the first-rate attention this brave son, and soon-to-be husband, deserves.

programme.title: What's In the Barn? - 12 Hours and Running
programme.desc: “12hrs. & Running”: Dale turns a surprise 60th birthday party into a challenge to his guests to start 120 of the rarest motorcycles in the world.

programme.title: What's In the Barn? - Hidden Hillclimbers
programme.desc: A cross-country race to lay claim to some of the world’s most sought after works specials.

programme.title: What's In the Barn? - Peashooter
programme.desc: A vague tip leads Matt and Dale into a WWII era military warehouse and beyond.

programme.title: What's In the Barn? - Davenport Swap
programme.desc: A thousand bulging barns of choppers, bobbers, and board trackers are emptied and on display in the Quad Cities.

programme.title: What's In the Barn? - 100 Year Old Harley
programme.desc: “Yes, Dale there is a 100 Year Old Harley”: Dale hopes his labor of love becomes a prized possession when a six-figure Harley goes to auction.

programme.title: What's In the Barn? - Motorcycle Crime Story
programme.desc: “A Motorcycle Crime Story”: The horrific tale of a double murder over money and motorcycles.

programme.title: What's In the Barn? - The Century Race
programme.desc: “The Century Race”: Dale believes he found the bike that will help him beat a rival in an annual race of 100 year old motorcycles. But will it run?

programme.title: What's In the Barn? - Doubling Down
programme.desc: A California collector gambles on a big payday from his Crocker/Caddy combo.

programme.title: Rutter's Detailin Garage - Detailing a Brand New Car Part 1
programme.desc: Nick Rutter, along with special guest Bruno Massel, walks you through step-by-step on how to perform a waterless wash, clay and polish, applying a sealant, and finishing with a topcoat wax on a brand-new car.

programme.title: Rutter's Detailin Garage - Interior Car Care
programme.desc: Nick Rutter provides valuable tips and techniques for cleaning and rejuvenating your cars interior

programme.title: Truck U - Save the Paint
programme.desc: Bruno takes a trip to Surf City Garage and learns some techniques from the pros that can help you save money on your next project.

programme.title: Truck U - Paid to Play
programme.desc: Matt and Bruno work on a truck that belongs to a tournament fisherman who uses his truck for work and play. It racks up a lot of miles. They are going to hook this boat captain up with some new products that will make his life a lot easier on the road!

programme.title: Truck U - Better Than Stock
programme.desc: Matt and Bruno are working on a 2006 Dodge Ram with a ton of problems…but why just make the repairs? Go better than stock!

programme.title: Truck U - Wheels For Hope
programme.desc: Matt visits the Wheels for Hope Garage in Raleigh, NC and sees first hand how this organization is changing lives.

programme.title: Truck U - Budget Rear End
programme.desc: Matt and Bruno upgrade the Dana 35 rear end in a 2000 Jeep TJ to a much stronger Ford 8.8 one and do it for under $500.

programme.title: Truck U - Warning Lights
programme.desc: Every light on your dashboard means something. Matt and Bruno diagnose and repair a vehicle that came to the shop to them with warning lights.

programme.title: Truck U - Road Ranger
programme.desc: Nobody likes to fix a truck on the side of the road. That’s why there’s roadside assistance. Matt and Bruno take a stock F250 and turn it into a purpose-built Road Ranger.

programme.title: Truck U - Jeep Build-Off Day 1
programme.desc: With two days and a ton of truck parts anything can happen. Matt and Bruno put together build teams to see who can complete the toughest rig.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - SMOKIN JOE
programme.desc: Blues legend Joe Bonamassa is looking for a surprise gift for his parents. With his love of cars and guitars, Joe has to get Ray and Mr. H involved. From Mopar muscle to Mr. H’s man cave to a concert performance from Joe, this episode has a lot of Drive.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - BOARD TRACK BATTLES
programme.desc: In the early 1900s, race tracks built of wood sprang up across the country drawing crowds of 50,000 and more. Ray and Xtreme sports superstar Travis Pastrana break down the Battle on the Boards.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - MODEL T
programme.desc: Ray finds an old T-Bucket that made a 133 mph pass at the Bonneville Salt Flats in 1951. The team brings the Woody Lee T back to life and attempts to match its original speed.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - DAN GURNEY
programme.desc: Ray finds a Gurney Eagle in a garage on Long Island. He wants to put the coveted machine back on the track at Indy to honor Dan Gurney. After intense restoration and scouring the globe for a Gurney-Westlake engine, the Eagle soars again.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - VINTAGE RACING
programme.desc: Ray uncovers the infamous “Brawner Hawk” – from the Dean Van Lines team – that became the breakout car in the career of Mario Andretti. Ray enlists HOF mechanic Jim McGee to restore the vehicle and, along the way, discovers a replica with many of the car’s original parts. After an award winning stop at Concours Amelia Island, the car heads to the 100th running of Indy 500 where Mario Andretti himself gets behind the wheel.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Super Cars at Course d'Elegance
programme.desc: One of a kind cars at Amelia Island Caffeine & Octane.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - A New Generation Takes over Amelia Island Caffeine & Octane
programme.desc: Caffeine & Octane at Ameilia Island have everything from classic to extraterrestrial.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Voices: Blackberry Smoke
programme.desc: A new season kicks off with some big numbers: Featuring the world's fastest C8 Corvette, an overpowered Cortina Mark I, and a Buick Regal with over 1,700 horsepower

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Voices: Bishop Rob Wright
programme.desc: C & O: North America's most diverse car show: Featuring custom Bugs, John Kaase fire breathing '56 Fords, and Chevy Silverados touching 1000HP

programme.title: Brumos Collection with Hurley
programme.desc: C & O Jacksonville: Featuring Hurley Haywood's Brumos Porsches, a street legal Indy car, and a dynamic '60 Olds

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Brumos Collection with Hurley
programme.desc: C & O Jacksonville: Featuring Hurley Haywood's Brumos Porsches, a street legal Indy car, and a dynamic '60 Olds

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Graffiti Artist KBMER
programme.desc: Jacksonville's C & O's colorful and quirky car culture.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Voices: Sport's Radio Legend, Mike Bell
programme.desc: C & O: North America's greatest car show

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Voices: Killer Mike
programme.desc: Caffeine & Octane: North America's greatest car show

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Fuller's Fab 5
programme.desc: Amelia Island's own Caffeine & Octane car show,: Featuring $2,000 Wrek Racing Truck, David Patterson's favorite C&O cars, a plastic Citroen Mehari, and much more.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Generation Amelia
programme.desc: The Concours d'Elegance from Amelia Island: Featuring bomb-proof Conquest Knight XV, '69 Camaro Hagerty Fixer-Upper, and car-enthusiast John Oates.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Delta Wing Racer with a Vengeance
programme.desc: State-of-the-art engineering and passion projects take center stage on this edition of Caffeine & Octane. The revolutionary Delta Wing Racer turns on hot lap after hot lap at Road Atlanta, and driver Katherine Legge offers insight on how “less is more” when it comes to power to weight ratios; the dark world of a graffiti artist paints a picture of a car called Vengeance while the hosts share the eclectic hybrids of machines that make it to Caffeine and Octane with Vengance’s creator Totem.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Henry Ford III & Counterfeit Cars
programme.desc: The word is out that Caffeine & Octane is the ultimate scene and the place to be seen. Even after 12 hours of racing at the Petit Le Mans endurance race, Henry Ford III, the great grandson of Henry Ford the industrial magnate and current member of Ford’s Board of Directors, showed up to take in the sights, address the crowds, and sign a one of a kind Ford Shelby GT350R. The GT350R will hit the road with Operation Hope to encourage young people to study math and science through motorsports; Covert car counterfeiter, Chuck Beck looks for Ford’s approval on his own version of a muscled up Festiva. YouTuber ‘That Dude in Blue’ David Patterson joins Skip and Bryan to get a special look at the motor-based philanthropic outreach and amazing cars that come with it done by John Hope Bryant, friend of Caffeine and Octane.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Go Fast Collections
programme.desc: Rickey and Bryan race outside of their comfort zones, each competing to find the raddest car collection. Rickey hangs out with classic racer, Ron Green, who shows him the ropes in the ultra light '83 Ralt RT5. Bryan visits a collection of 120 racing motorcycle owned by mechanic and racer Mike McConville. Mike takes Bryan motorcross racing in his backyard after showing off his high horsepowered car collection. Caffeine and Octane alongside Ron Green visit Atlanta's first Concours d'elegance to look at classic cars that have been perfectly maintained for decades.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - The Wrap on the Most Beautiful Cars
programme.desc: Wayne Phears’ Jaguar E-Type was nothing more than a diamond in the rough before the mineral rights attorney took on the basket case. Caffeine & Octane brings viewers the story of how Phears’, through relentless research and determination, restored and reunited the #10 Jag that Enzo Ferrari called “the most beautiful car ever made”; Atlanta Wraps shows how wrapping Corvettes and Lamborghinis can color a car fast. Caffeine and Exotics is featured again to show off the coolest wraps Atlanta can supply.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Panoz Racing & Chasing Chevys
programme.desc: Twenty-five years of hand building record-setting race cars is no easy task. But don’t tell that to Don Panoz who takes on Porsches and Ferraris with his laid back personality and a full-production facility that makes improvised versions of Americas V-8’s; Caffeine & Octane gets an exclusive look at an elusive man’s life long pursuit of everything Chevrolet.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Retrofitting a Psycho Mercury
programme.desc: Caffeine & Octane goes old school with Psycho Deville singer and pin striper Walt Richards. Richards has less than a week to hand paint his signature flames on a way cool ’50 Mercury. An introduction to Retrofit Lighting’s trickle down technology - from cars to motorcycles - that will revolutionize how bikes will see and be seen.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Beetles, Bugs, and Buses, Oh My!
programme.desc: Caffeine & Octane is known for having something for everyone. That reputation is tested when over 100 VW’s, on the lunatic fringe of automotive, rattled into an event. The annual VW Invasion has swarmed C&O, bringing with it Bugs, Beetles, Buses and more by a very dedicated VW fan base.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Big Tigger v. Monster Muscle
programme.desc: Rapper, radio, and ATL TV personality Darian “Big Tigger” Morgan invites Caffeine & Octane into his home. Cameras look past his collection of 500 Michael Jordan shoes to focus their lenses on a one-off motorcycle the 6–time NBA Champion gave to Tigger as a gift; A second to none, muscle car collection by one of the founders of Monster Energy gets toured by one of our hosts.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Exotic Car Hunter
programme.desc: Vintage Testarossas and Quatra Valve Ferraris don’t just show up on a dealer’s floor, they’re hunted! Adam Merlin, exotic car expert, has spent a lifetime searching for the cars that defined his youth, but nothing is assumed or given in his high stakes game of Risk; A muscle car fiend shows off his private collection to host Skip Smith.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Andruw Jones' Super Jeep
programme.desc: The word is out that Caffeine & Octane is the major league of North American car shows. Former Atlanta Brave, World Series star, and 10-Time Gold Glove winner Andruw Jones acknowledged the stardom and showed up to show off his 6 wheeled Super Jeep. The state of the art creation took years in planning and execution, but Jones chooses one great event for its debut.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Jekyll and Hide
programme.desc: The natural landscape of Jekyll Island, GA makes for a great backdrop to showcase the country’s greatest cars. But the ocean air and remoteness of this GA barrier island is no place to leave a painstakingly rebuilt ‘59 Imperial Speedster. Host Skip Smith makes it his mission to locate the talented builder behind this island mystery. Guest host Totem joins Bryan Fuller in the C&O lots to pick out his favorites.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Big Yellow Taxi
programme.desc: Imagination, ingenuity, and heartache join friends who set out with a simple goal of transforming a ‘35 Plymouth into a running taxi cab. Provenance and passion take center stage as families and friends strive for best in show. Host Rickey Gadson checks out perfect replicas of the original Shelby Cobras and takes Skip and Bryan on ‘borrowed’ taxi ride.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Peach State Challengers
programme.desc: Call the Peach State Challengers a “car club” and you will quickly be corrected. The defenders of everything Dodge are “family” and they make no excuses for their loyalty to brand and brother. Spend a day at the track with these Challengers and find out what their version of the family photo looks like.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Lambo Racer
programme.desc: Former Jet Ski Champion Ryan Hardwick has succeeded in everything he’s touched in racing and business, so why not race Lamborghinis! Hardwick has found rapid success with his high tech training methods; he shares his secrets with host Rickey Gadson in this episode of C&O.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Boston's (Un)Common Collection
programme.desc: Daimlers and Morgans, and Citroens, oh my! Caffeine & Octane is known for having something for everyone. That reputation grows stronger as Bob Boston’s collection, on the lunatic fringe of automotive, rattles into an event; guest host Katherine Legge explains the competitor to her racing sponsor Acura, Porsche.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - C&O at the Beach
programme.desc: Early spring in Jekyll Island, GA is something to behold. Add a few hundred of the country’s coolest rides for Caffeine & Octane at the Beach, and it’s a thing of beauty. Models that blasted the glass ceiling of expectation at the all makes and models show include a hand-built, twin-turbo Tucker, a one-off 1951 Kaiser Drag’N, and a Roadkill Custom known only as Blasphemi!

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Kenny Rogers' Family Values
programme.desc: Caffeine & Octane is back at the beach on Jekyll Island with a focus on cars and families. Fathers & sons, with their barn-find Pilot House trucks and Plymouth Satellites, call cars the glue that keeps them bonded together with C&O being the best place to show them off.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - SEMA: Inside Fuller's Head
programme.desc: On this edition of Caffeine & Octane, we’ll go inside the head of Master Builder Bryan Fuller at the SEMA car show, the largest annual trade show in the country; SEMA always brings out the best in the automotive industry. From a jaw dropping Javelin to a multimillion-dollar Pagani, Brian will prove to you, what happens in Vegas, doesn’t always stay in Vegas! Ryan Hardwick guest hosts and ventures deep into the C&O lots to find his favorite ride of the day.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Motor Masters
programme.desc: Take apart and assemble a motor in less than 30 minutes? Sure, NASCAR pit crews can make the claim, but can a rag tag group of kids with names like Jelly Donut, Butter Bean, and Wonder Woman claim the pit fame? Host Rickey Gadson heads to the Sims Academy to settle a bet. This ultra fast half-hour edition of Caffeine & Octane also includes a builder’s take on an Ultra Four Unlimited Off-Road Racer; Ryan Hardwick joins the gang at Caffeine and Octane.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Viva Cuba!
programme.desc: Demetrio Montalvo likes American music, American food, and especially American cars. Only one problem - Demetrio lives in Cuba. A Visa, bestowed to him from some American friends in high places, lets him travel to the States to search for tough to find automotive parts and attend his favorite car show. Showing up to Caffeine & Octane in a cool caddy is the easy part. Picking parts to take back home presents a unique set of challenges for the Cuban car builder; Formula Drift racer Federico Sceriffo brings the first ever Formula Drift Ferrari to C&O.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Magnus Walker's Secret Porsche Collection
programme.desc: Porsche collector, customizer, and Urban Outlaw, Magnus Walker is a world-wide rock star to fans of the German car company. So, when Caffeine & Octane producers invited him to travel cross country to tell the story of a hidden Porsche collection tangled in a North Georgia Vineyard, they were surprised when he signed on. No sour grapes here, what Walker found at the Wolf Mountain Winery will impress even those with the most refined palette for Porsches.

programme.title: Rutter's Detailin Garage - Exterior Car Care
programme.desc: Nick Rutter provides valuable tips and techniques for properly washing your vehicle to avoid instilling swirl marks

programme.title: Rutter's Detailin Garage - Restoring A Used Car
programme.desc: Nick Rutter teaches how to restore headlights, clean engine, remove water spots and other tips on a used car

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - 2011 Mustang
programme.desc: Kevin takes us through variable cam timing in Ford’s new 5.0L power plant.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - Café Racer Gets Jets
programme.desc: Fuller and Byrd bring back the Café Racer Honda bike and show you how to open up the jets in the carburetor for better performance.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - Turbo Technology
programme.desc: We look at the technology behind turbochargers and intercoolers as we install some upgrades on a F250 Super Duty.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - Operation Comfort
programme.desc: Bryan takes a trip to San Antonio to teach some of our nations heroes how to weld.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - Cold Air, Hot Rod
programme.desc: We tackle an A/C cooling problem on a Toyota Tacoma and introduce you to Kevin’s Hot Rod BMW.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - LSM3
programme.desc: Kevin and Bryan continue the work on Kevin’s LSM3 project.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - Inside the Cat
programme.desc: Kevin travels to Indiana to solve the mysteries of the catalytic converter.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - Cool Tools
programme.desc: Kevin and Bryan show some cool tools and shop tips while working on Kevin’s Hot Rod BMW.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Put Up Your Dukes
programme.desc: Mark isolates himself in the machine-shop and begins the assembly of the 440 4BBL for the 1970 Plum Crazy Challenger previously owned by a former cast member.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - General Deliver Lee
programme.desc: It’s a delivery day at Graveyard Carz! We jump back and show you every moment in the restoration of our legendary, screen-used, record holding, General Lee from the 2005 Dukes of Hazzard film. It all builds to a reveal the Duke Boys would be proud of.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Inner Workings of a Victim
programme.desc: With all the distractions out of the way, Mark returns to the machine-ship to finish the 440 4BBL for the 1970 Plum Crazy Challenger while master-assembler Dave begins working on the 1970 Lemon-Twist Roadrunner.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Dueling Drivetrains
programme.desc: With Mark out of the shop looking at a potential new location for a Mopar car-dealership, the Graveyard Ghouls begin the drivetrain assembly’s for a 1970 Lime Light Tribute Superbird and our 1970 Plum Crazy Challenger.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Getting Schooled
programme.desc: Mark the the Graveyard Ghouls race against the clock to finish up some last-minute tasks on their various Mopar restorations before traveling north to judge Little Creek Casino’s epic car show.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Roadrunner With a Twist
programme.desc: It’s another reveal day! Mark and Allysa install the graphics on the ’71 Plumb Crazy Challenger and the 1970 Lemon-Twist Roadrunner is finished and readied to be delivered to it’s owner. And Mark proposes - what he thinks is - a “brilliant” idea to the producers of the show.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Engines, Assemblies, and Dougie, Oh My!
programme.desc: With our 1970 Plum Crazy Challenger nearing completion, works shifts to our 1970 Tribute Superbird while Mark and Cousin Dougie build out a 340 engine and Will assists with metal work with George to help him stay on schedule.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - I Love the Smell of Plum Crazy in the Morning
programme.desc: It’s a special day at Graveyard Carz. The 1970 Plum Crazy Challenger that has been part of our show since its inception is finally finished and ready to be shipped to it’s new owner. But before they do that, they take it to the coast to show it off at Chinook Winds Casino Resort Car Show!

programme.title: Gone in 60 Days
programme.desc: Work ramps up on our 1970 Tribute Superbird and just when things couldn’t get more stressful, Mark has taken on a new project - a 1969 GTX owned by non other than Chris Jacobs, host of Overhaulin’. Not only does Chris want the car restored to its former glory, he wants it to be completed in time for SEMA 2017 - only sixty days away!

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Mopardy
programme.desc: Stress is high at the shop and things don’t get any better when Mark finds out that Tony from Tony’s Mopar Parts has secretly flown out to check in on the progress of his 1970 Challenger. Knowing that the restoration hasn’t started, Mark does everything in his power to distract Tony while Cousin Dougie races to disassemble his car.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - A Buck Shorted
programme.desc: SEMA 2017 is fast approaching and the Graveyard Ghouls are working hard on Chris Jacob’s 1969 GTX and John Buck’s 1971 Challenger with a Hellcrate Hemi. But as pressure begins to mount, Mark questions if he’ll be able to keep his promised - and ultimately - is forced to make a difficult decision.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - The Phoenix 'Cuda
programme.desc: The Phoenix Cuda: A legendary Mopar which was incinerated and from the ashes, will be reborn at Graveyard Carz.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - SEMA Has Eyes On A Hellbird
programme.desc: Graveyard Carz returns to conclude the epic builds of Chris Jacob’s 1968 GTX after it suffered a devastating fire, and the first of its kind 1970 Plymouth Superbird equipped with a Hellcat Crate Hemi.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - 68 GTX vs Echo Bomber
programme.desc: New Season, New Carz! With SEMA in their rear-view mirror, the ghouls begin the final assembly of a 1969 Plymouth GTX in Seafoam Turquoise. And when help comes up short-handed, Mark enlists what he likes to call his “Echo Bomber.”

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Pilot Takes Flight
programme.desc: It’s the 100th Episode! Join Mark and Royal and watch the never-before-aired Pilot featuring some of the first Mopar restorations the original ghouls tackled over ten-years ago.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Surveying The Will To Work
programme.desc: It’s a hectic day at the Graveyard. Dave jumps from car to car, installing and assembling what he can, Will struggles to keep focused on one project at a time, and Mark makes progress on his A100 build with the hopes of revealing at SEMA 2018!

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - What's Next?
programme.desc: Professional wrestler, actor, and former professional football player known for his time in the WWE and WCW as Goldberg returns to the Graveyard! This time, he brings the ghouls two brand new projects - one of which includes installing a brand-new 392 Crate Hemi in his modern Dodge Truck.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Performance with a Touch of Luxury
programme.desc: The Ghouls say goodbye to the 1970 Superbird equipped with a Hellcat Crate Hemi by taking it out for one last drive. Then it’s all hands on deck to get the beautiful second-generation Plymouth GTX in Seafoam Turquoise finished in time for the owner to see it for the first time since his father drove it.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Dissecting a Daytona
programme.desc: With a Plymouth GTX finished, the ghoulz refocus their efforts on the 1969 Dodge Daytona. With the owner wanting a full restoration, Mark and the Ghouls begin the tedious disassembly. Because of the car’s pristine condition, they take extra effort to document and preserve as much as they can.

programme.title: Rutter's Detailin Garage - Speciality Car Care
programme.desc: Nick Rutter teaches how to restore interior plastic trim, glass cleaning, convertible top care and more

programme.title: Rutter's Detailin Garage - Paint Scratch Repair
programme.desc: Nick Rutter teaches how to repair common scratches in automotive paint

programme.title: Truck U - Jeep Build-Off Day 2
programme.desc: The Jeep Build-Off continues as the guys get a chance to finish their Jeeps and take them on a Geocaching race for all of the marbles.

programme.title: Truck U - Jeep Build-Off Day 3
programme.desc: The Jeep Build-Off continues as the guys get a chance to finish their Jeeps and take them on a Geocaching race for all of the marbles.

programme.title: Truck U - Jeep Build-Off Day 4
programme.desc: The Jeep Build-Off continues as the guys get a chance to finish their Jeeps and take them on a Geocaching race for all of the marbles.

programme.title: Truck U - Jeep Build-Off Day 5
programme.desc: The Jeep Build-Off continues as the guys get a chance to finish their Jeeps and take them on a Geocaching race for all of the marbles.

programme.title: Truck U - Commander vs Razor
programme.desc: What UTV should you buy? Should it be a Can Am Commander or a Razor RZR? Matt and Bruno show you what kind of upgrades you can do to each of them, and then they have some fun on the trails!

programme.title: Truck U - Shop Misinformation
programme.desc: Matt and Bruno show you what a car dealership is going to look for when your truck hits a 100-thousand miles to determine its trade in value. Matt and Bruno show you what kind of repairs you can do yourself to get the most value on your truck.

programme.title: Truck U - Diesel Track Truck
programme.desc: The gloves are off….so is the exhaust, DPF, and anything else that makes our diesel track truck street legal. Today a King Ranch is getting ready for a date with the drag strip.

programme.title: Truck U - Wish List Project
programme.desc: A final wish list from a customer gets completed on a 1985 C-10 project truck, and we cure a slipping transmission problem on a Dodge.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - TWO JUNIORS
programme.desc: Dale Earnhardt Jr. wants Ray to help restore an El Camino, except add on a Laguna front end. It’s not a typical restoration, but it’s certainly a NASCAR performance-worthy restoration. Ray and the team build the car and then, in a bit of gamesmanship, challenge Kyle Tucker, owner of Detroit Speed, to a series of races against his award winning Camaro. But as always, Ray has an ace up his sleeve in Al Unser, Jr. as his driver.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - MACHINE MAGIC MARIO
programme.desc: In the late 60s, Camaros, Mustangs, and Corvettes roared to the forefront of the muscle car world. When Ray gets offered a spot in a vintage race featuring these cars, he’s propelled to dive into their history and afforded a chance to get behind the wheel at the Brickyard. But because it is a pro-am race, Ray pulls in a partner – none other than Bill Elliott, aka “Awesome Bill from Dawsonville.”

programme.title: AmeriCarna - HOLLYWOOD MAKEOVER
programme.desc: Everyone has a car they fell in love with. Maybe it was the first? Maybe it was the hot rod the guy down the street owned? In Ray’s case, it was the ’58 Chevy he saw in the iconic coming of age car movie, American Graffiti. When Ray gets the chance to own the car after years and years of pursuit, he jumps on it. But restoring the car to its "movie state" proves to be a grueling task that goes way beyond a typical build. But when it's you first love, you can't let her down.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - WOODIE WAGONS
programme.desc: The premiere episode features a dive into the influence cars had on culture of California. From road racing in the 1940’s to the inception of the iconic Woody wagon on the California beach scene, automobiles undoubtedly shaped the West Coast attitude. Ray talks to The Beach Boys frontman Mike Love and surfing icon Laird Hamilton for a glimpse into the cars that are so connected to surfing culture.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - MOONSHINE
programme.desc: Ray uncovers the secrets of America’s greatest racing sport in the foothills of North Carolina. When moonshiners were looking to outrun police officers, they would custom fit engines to race the back woods tracks. That sport eventually evolved into NASCAR and Ray talks to the early adopters of this national pastime.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - MARTY ROBBINS
programme.desc: Grammy-winning musician Marty Robbins lived a double life, flawlessly crossing between the glitz of the Grand Ole Opry and track at Talladega. One of few musicians to ever achieve success in both genres, Ray is determined to return Marty’s iconic 777 car to the race track travel back to the 60’s and 70’s.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - HOT RODS
programme.desc: Legendary drag racer Don “The Snake” Prudhomme proves he is no stranger to the thrill of racing rivalry. Prudhomme’s career and subsequent competition with driver Tom “The Mongoose” McEwan earned the pair an unforgettable imprint on American pop culture. Legions of kids in the 60’s and 70’s flocked to the shelves for Snake and Mongoose Matchbox cars which helped make drag racing an undisputable American pastime. Famed director Ron Howard makes an appearance to discuss the incredible stories hidden within the world of Formula 1 Racing, the topic of his latest, epic feature ‘Rush”.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Chipper Jones' International Car Show
programme.desc: A Hall of Famer?s search for an Exotic Car that fits; a blind man?s ?touchy feely? approach to seeing great cars.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Fuller's "2029" Majestic Build
programme.desc: Back to the future with a Majestic build from Fuller Moto that showcases 3-D printed parts, an electric motor, and hub-centric steering.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - The Fastest Man on Two Wheels
programme.desc: Horsepower and million-dollar sticker shock, from two and four wheels, dominate this edition of C & O.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Power Play: Proline's Search for 3000+ HP
programme.desc: A need for speed in a Sleepy Georgia town has engineers pushing the boundaries to exceed 3000 horsepower.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Turn of the Century Cars
programme.desc: Turn of the century cars?with wooden wheels?at 85mph?oh my!

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Hot Wheel's & an Urban Outlaw
programme.desc: Urban Outlaw and designer Magnus Walker weighs in on his beloved Porsche and the legend of Hot Wheels.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Proving Provenance on a One-Off Ferrari
programme.desc: Tracing the origins and solving the mystery of a controversial all-aluminum bodied 512 BBI Boxer Ferrari.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Twin City Turbos
programme.desc: Caffeine & Octane goes Turbo in the Twin Cities; Ferrari Drifter Federico Sceriffo finds a home in Atlanta.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - BURIED TREASURE
programme.desc: Ray finds Buried Treasures in the form of two race cars that had been left almost completely untouched since the last time they were on a race track. One is found sitting in a barn with the other trapped in an old car hauler. Upon uncovering the cars, we find both have big stories to tell.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - INDY RESCUE
programme.desc: A journey to Indianapolis unveils the winning car of the 1955 Indy 500 sits on the roof of an auto mechanic shop. More than 50 years after it’s magic at the track, prominent Indy mechanic/owner Chris Paulsen begins to restore the car and Ray talks about the evolution of America’s illustrious Indy 500.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - SMOKEY
programme.desc: In the 1960s, Smokey Yunick, the most innovative mechanic in NASCAR history, sold his famous truck that hauled equipment & cars across the country. Ray is on a mission to uncover this storied truck that helped launch the beginning of NASCAR’s cross-country popularity.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - VANDERBILT
programme.desc: Ray travels to Long Island, New York with the hopes of learning more about America’s first major auto racing competition. The race, which began at the turn of the 20th century, helped develop the first major American racing stars and drove American manufacturing to competitive standard with the highly progressive Europeans.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - RAYS ROOTS
programme.desc: Fred Dmuchowski is a New Jersey racing icon. He was a regular workingman who raced on weekends for fun and became a legend in his iconic 303 racer. Ray works to rebuild the 303 in homage to Fred who inspired him to pursue his career in motorsports.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - THE CORVETTE
programme.desc: For Rick Hendrick, the Corvette inspired his first car purchase, which ultimately led to the creation of his car dealership empire and motorsports teams. Mr. H, as he goes by, gives us a rare look inside of his personal Corvette collection and takes us to General Motors World Headquarters to talk with GM Vice President of Global Design, Ed Welburn, about how this iconic car came about. Country superstar Brad Paisley makes a cameo appearance to talk about his prized ‘Vette and the influence of cars on his music.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - SECRETS OF GM
programme.desc: General Motors VP of global design Ed Welburn takes AmeriCarna behind the scenes to talk about the history and development of the company’s concept cars that never made it to production. In a rare tour of the factory’s inner design studio, Ray gets an exclusive look behind the creative curtain at the GM plant in Detroit.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - FIRST MUSTANG
programme.desc: In this episode, Ray celebrates the 50th birthday of the Ford Mustang through the lens of three distinct Mustang owners, who each hold a slice of the car’s incredible history. In 1964, Chicago schoolteacher Gail Wise unknowingly purchased the first ever Mustang 3 days before the car became available to the general public. Her husband reveals the incredible restoration that brought the earliest Mustang ever sold back to its 60’s glory. (Ford Motor Company knows she has the car and has verified it was sold before it was legal to sell.) John Grafelman is a farmer in Illinois and life-long Ford lover. A few years ago he found an old car hidden in a barn. It took some time, but he eventually convinced his friend to sell it to him. After some detective work, he discovers he purchased 1968 Ford Design Chief Larry Shinoda’s personal car that he tinkered with to invent the BOSS 302.Lastly, Ray travels to Dayton Ohio to interview Gale Holderman, the Ford stylist who won the company’s contest to draw and design the Mustang.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - 470MPH with Custom King George Poteet
programme.desc: Jaguar F-Type Project 7 crosses the Pond.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Art & Oddities at Old Car City, USA
programme.desc: Mustang Mayhem at the big show.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - The Unsung Hero in the Movie Ford v. Ferrari
programme.desc: Red carpet receptions for a Koenigsegg Regera.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Hollywood's Cars Come to Life at Picture Car Productions
programme.desc: Covert cars that slipped into the U.S.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Rickey Gadson Stares Down a Steep Learning Curve at Rd. Atl
programme.desc: "Go Fast Girl" Katherne Legge's Model A Racer.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - A Sneak Peek at a Premier, Private Collection
programme.desc: Mecum Auction's push for a $15,000,000 sale.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - The World's Largest Monthly Car Show Goes Up for Sale to the Highest Bidder
programme.desc: Doubling down for cars and coffee.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Landscaping a Home with a Nod to Automotive
programme.desc: British Invasion through the decades.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - FORGOTTEN FIBERGLASS
programme.desc: Post-World War II, a large generation of American soldiers was looking for cars that mirrored the incredible sports cars they saw overseas in Europe. After searching for an affordable way to recreate these cars, one manufacturer responded with a durable, cost effective option: a line of fiberglass sports cars. Very few of these cars still exist but there are collectors who are constantly looking to save parts and pieces of these virtually unknown vehicles. We learn about these rare cars from Geoff Hacker and his team at Forgotten Fiberglass, and take a visit to the Smithsonian Museum, which houses a fiberglass car of its own. This episode presents a unique look at how the narrative of American wartime experiences often shaped the nation’s automotive demands.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - DISCOVERIES IN JERSEY
programme.desc: Ray explores buying a unique, hand built car the Schultz family has kept in their New Jersey home for years. The car was originally called the Schultz El Fierro but it’s officially titled in New Jersey as a “Schultz Special” from 1958. It was used to drag race and held numerous racing records, but most important to the family was that it was driven by the legendary Carroll Shelby. With a frame and style much similar to the Shelby Roadster, the Schultz family wonders if it inspired one of the most well known automobiles in America. In another epic New Jersey story, Ray finds Mario Andretti’s 1965 IndyCar. The catch: it’s completely in pieces hidden away in a garage. The iconic driver talks about his days on the track with that car and Ray attempts to negotiate a deal to buy the parts.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - MOVIE CARS
programme.desc: How do you cast a car in a movie? What are the secrets to the build? Hollywood car designers give Ray the ultimate tour of some memorable movie cars, including a look at the recently found “Hells Chariot”, the last surviving car from Grease. We also hear the backstory to “Bumble Bee” from Transformers, take a look at the making of the tanks for Hunger Games and stumble upon the iconic 1958 Chevy Impala from American Graffiti.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - HOT ROD HOLY GRAIL
programme.desc: While visiting the estate of automotive genius and land speed record holder Willie Glass, Ray uncovers a set of extremely rare Ardun cylinder heads, which is a hot rodders dream discovery. He also visits the infamous H & H Flatheads outside of Los Angeles and discovers a surprise connection to the Earnhardt family.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - CORVETTE SINK HOLE
programme.desc: A massive sinkhole opened underneath the National Corvette Museum in Kentucky. Eight amazing cars were swallowed up, including the 1,000,000th Corvette. AmeriCarna was given exclusive access to document the removal of the cars from the sinkhole and the painstaking restoration of the 1,000,000th Corvette.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - ROAR AT THE SHORE
programme.desc: On the beaches of Wildwood, NJ, an iconic vintage race, The Race of Gentleman, takes place annually: period cars, period dress, pure authenticity. Ray puts a car in the race with a very special driver, who turns out not to be a Gentleman.

programme.title: AmeriCarna - CARS AND MUSIC STARS
programme.desc: Scott Borchetta, CEO and Founder of Big Machine Label Group, and his brother, Mark, love racing just as much as anyone. From Indy to Nashville, Ray finds he has a lot in common with the Borchetta brothers. This celebrity-filled episode features musicians Tim McGraw, Cassadee Pope and IndyCar driver Josef Newgarden.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - The Fertile Car Grounds in the North Georgia Mountains
programme.desc: Due diligence on custom builds and their quirky builders.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Cars and Coffee Takes Center Stage on Amelia Island
programme.desc: The Concourse d'lemons celebrates the oddball, mundane, & truly awful of the automotive world.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Concourse d'lemons: One Man's Trash Is a Car Enthusiast's Treasure
programme.desc: Rickey, Bryan and Adam explore all the cars that should never have been built at Concourse d'lemons.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Super Cars at Course d'Elegance
programme.desc: One of a kind cars at Amelia Island Caffeine & Octane.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - A New Generation Takes over Amelia Island Caffeine & Octane
programme.desc: Caffeine & Octane at Ameilia Island have everything from classic to extraterrestrial.

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Voices: Blackberry Smoke
programme.desc: A new season kicks off with some big numbers: Featuring the world's fastest C8 Corvette, an overpowered Cortina Mark I, and a Buick Regal with over 1,700 horsepower

programme.title: Caffeine & Octane - Voices: Bishop Rob Wright
programme.desc: C & O: North America's most diverse car show: Featuring custom Bugs, John Kaase fire breathing '56 Fords, and Chevy Silverados touching 1000HP

programme.title: Brumos Collection with Hurley
programme.desc: C & O Jacksonville: Featuring Hurley Haywood's Brumos Porsches, a street legal Indy car, and a dynamic '60 Olds

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Exorcize a Demon
programme.desc: The unsung heroes of the body shop begin the restoration on one of the most requested renovations ever: a 1971 Dodge Demon. With several panels needing replacement, Mark and George show the intricate process of grafting an entire front-clip from another vehicle to replace the rusted and damaged original.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Frankenstein's Work
programme.desc: It’s crunch time at Graveyard Carz, Mark and his cousin Dougie fire up the engine for our Turbine Bronze Charger while Dave assembles its firewall in preparation to install the drivetrain.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Look to the West for B5 Blue
programme.desc: Dave is ready to wrap up the 1970 Challenger R/T SE but is waiting for parts. Meanwhile, Mark’s A100 build continues, and after a long and tedious body-work and paint phase, Will is ready to deliver Dave the 1969 B5 Blue Plymouth GTX.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - A Challenger Reflected
programme.desc: The Ghoulz have finished the 1970 Challenger R/T SE. As they take it out for the first test drive in over 15 years, Mark and the team reflect on the trials and challenges throughout the restoration process.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Mark, Dougie, Allysa, Oh My!
programme.desc: Mark, Dougie, and Allysa team up to kick things into high-gear around the shop. They begin the restoration of a Challenger T/A, Will paints a 1968 GTX in B7 Blue, Allysa learns how dashes are assembled, and they install the drivetrain for the ultra rare, 1 of 2, 1970 Hemi Convertible Coronet R/T.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Shaking the Magic 8-Ball
programme.desc: Mark pulls back the curtain on the next four Mopar’s in desperate need of resurrection while also showcasing the fans favorite moments from Seasons 8 and 9.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Rapping Up the Dash
programme.desc: Mark and the Ghouls are back, and they are ready to get to work! With a call in from the owner of the super rare 1 of 2 ever made, 1970 Coronet Hemi Convertible, Mark has to work fast to reach a looming deadline. At the same time, Will primes the body of our 1969 Daytona, bringing it one step closer to being back on the road.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: While Mark is on a road test of the just completed 1968 Charger, its owner surprises the shop by arriving in person! While the owner waits, Will completes the paint on a beautiful 1970 Challenger in B5 blue. Finally, with Mark returned the owner receives his restored Charger and cooks a meal for the Ghouls as thanks!

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Upshift Creek
programme.desc: It’s time to hit the casino! Mark has been invited to a massive car show at the Little Creek Casino but before he can go there are a few things that need to be finished first. Mark and Doug disassemble the Phoenix ‘Cuda, a car that survived a massive garage fire. At the car show, Allysa and Will interact with fans and find their “pick of the show.”

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - It's A Duster Buster
programme.desc: Mark has once again taken Allysa under his wing and is teaching her the finer points of installing a grill and headlights on our 1972 Duster, meanwhile George is in the body shop attaching a tricky quarter panel to a 1969 ‘Cuda which Mark has to check off the list…military style? Finally, the Duster gets it’s drive train and is finally reunited with its engine.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Carbituary: Charger
programme.desc: Who better to explain the history and discuss the changes, year to year, of the Dodge Charger, than the Master of Mopar himself, Mark Worman. In this special episode of Graveyard Carz, Mark will show you unique differences, surprising insights, and cool features on multiple years of the same car: the Dodge Charger.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - 1.5 Million Dollar Baby
programme.desc: The focus has shifted, with parts having arrived at the shop it’s time to install the Grill, Bumper, Dash and Interior of our 1968 Turbine Bronze Charger. Mark and the Ghouls work their magic on the Charger until a familiar face shows up. With Royal there, Mark shifts the crew back to the 1 of 2 1970 Coronet and finishes the interior. Then, it’s time for a test drive!

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Willie, Paint it Black?
programme.desc: With SEMA 2018 looming on the horizon, it’s time to address the elephant in the room. Mark’s 1964 A100 Hemi truck, the Little Dead Wagon, built as an homage to Bill Maverick’s Little Red Wagon. Will is set to prime and paint, but has a disagreement with Mark over the color. When the engine for the A100 shows up it’s time to unbox one of the most powerful engines Graveyard Carz has ever put in a car!

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - The A100 Team
programme.desc: Mark and the Ghouls get down to work like never before in order to get the A100 ready for SEMA which is only weeks away. Brakes, suspension, tail lights, bed sealer, you name it and the Mopar Maniacs are racing to get it done. With time flying and stress mounting the team works long hours and weekends trying to finish this iconic build. With SEMA only days away, the A100 finally gets it’s iconic 426 HEMI engine, well, if they can make it fit!

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Night of the Little Dead Wagon
programme.desc: There is no time left, SEMA is almost here but the iconic A100 still needs some buttoning up. Installing all of the final components is an all hands on deck task and when the dust settles, Mark has only the decals left to put on the car, completing its legendary look. With the A100 finished the team heads out to SEMA in order to roll the Little Dead Wagon over the red carpet for Mopar and Mark once again receives a very kind gift from our friends.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Carbiturary: Cuda
programme.desc: When Mark get’s started talking about a car there is almost no stopping him. So it is again when Mark picks multiple years of a favorite Mopar of his and tells both the audience, and whoever will listen all about their amazing options and cool differences. The Ghouls join in on the fun just like last time to pick their favorite things as well. Don’t worry, though, he can’t talk forever right?

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Duster In The Wind
programme.desc: With SEMA over the Ghoulz can finally rest and get back to work on all the cars they had to leave untouched for the last few weeks…again. First up on deck is our beautiful 1972 Duster. Needing its Dash installed as well as its interior there is still a lot of work left on this car! Additionally, metal work has begun on a host of new cars moving up through the shop, it never stops at Graveyard Carz.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - B5: Before & After
programme.desc: A 1970 Challenger is a special thing, but a B5 Challenger is even more special. With the bumblebee stripe finally added to the car it’s time for the work to really begin. Work begins on all the things that make this 1970 Challenger special, including the headliner, dash, interior and more. With that done it’s time for this beautiful Challenger to get its 440 Six Pack!

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Emma's Car
programme.desc: The crew is overwhelmed with several restorations at once. From painting stripes, to engine parts, to missing parts on the 71 Charger, tensions are high around the shop. To Make things worse, Mark is obsessed with finding a factory pink Mopar for his granddaughter's 16th birthday.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - It's the Great Pumpkin, Mark Worman!
programme.desc: The crew at Graveyard Carz are excited about the pumpkin carving contest, but when a mistake leaves the 'Cuda filled with water, the whole crew has to pay the price. Royal saves the day, but with the carving contest back on the table, where are the pumpkins? Mark has to swallow his pride and accept help from Daren for the contest to go ahead.

programme.title: Graveyard Carz - Daren Goes Plum Crazy
programme.desc: Daren is given control of the shop for a week. Darenʼs constant pestering and ribbing leads Mark to hand him the reigns of Graveyard Carz for a week. But instead of getting things done, Daren starts inventing new field trips and distractions for the crew, as well as becoming obsessed with getting his personal Dodge Challenger running again.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - Boating Maintenance
programme.desc: Kevin services a tournament series high performance boat while Bryan welds up a new casting platform.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - Monster Transmission Auto Show
programme.desc: Kevin and Bryan hit the road and take in a classic auto show and take a tour at Monster Transmission and Performance.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - Power Steering Upgrade
programme.desc: Kevin and Bryan show us the proper way to upgrade the power steering system on an older vehicle and offer some other useful maintenance tips.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - Open House at Surf City
programme.desc: Bryan takes a trip to Huntington Beach, CA to learn some new detailing tips from the pros.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - F Sport Performance Upgrades
programme.desc: Kevin and Bryan take a Lexus IS250 and add the latest FSport performance products from front to back - taking IS performance to the next level.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - 2004 Corvette Project
programme.desc: Ken Schrader drops off a 2004 Corvette that is in need of some TLC.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - G-Oil
programme.desc: Kevin calls on his friends at Green Earth Technologies to learn about G-Oil and see how it is made.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - Pace Setter
programme.desc: Kevin and Bryan throw a couple hundred horses at a 2010 Camaro pace car with a new supercharger.

programme.title: Truck U - Wrecked
programme.desc: When your work truck gets wrecked, your ability to work stops. Matt and Bruno take on a project to get a work truck back on the road with some body work and repair.

programme.title: Truck U - Jeep Beast
programme.desc: Matt and Bruno ‘s Jeep project is going to be a beast when they get finished with it. The guys install a supercharger and a long arm suspension to give it some life, and they finish it up with a new exhaust and a mean front bumper that will provide the “bite.” Then, the beast is unleashed!

programme.title: Truck U - Build Stage CJ
programme.desc: Matt and Bruno do some custom fabrication to a build stage CJ 7. They also run new fuel lines and drop in a new fuel tank.

programme.title: Truck U - Fuel Delivery System
programme.desc: A lot can wrong in a Diesel trucks fuel delivery system. Today we look at corrosion in the lines and stuck injectors, to see if we can solve the problems with a secret.

programme.title: Truck U - Roush Truck
programme.desc: We love bolt on power, so today is going to be fun. We've got a Roush Supercharger kit, with an exhaust and intake to install on this stock 2012 F150. Then we’ll put it on the road and burn some rubber.

programme.title: Truck U - TJ Off-Road Upgrade
programme.desc: Matt and Bruno take a stock TJ and make it into the machine it was born to be. Once we upgrade the Steering and Suspension, we’ll make some much needed repair.

programme.title: Truck U - Can Am Fix
programme.desc: We bring our 2011 Cam Am Commander back for some final fixes. We beef up the steering with an electric power steering kit and throw some light on the subject with a large LED Light bar.

programme.title: Truck U - Paint Shop Chevy
programme.desc: This cool blue 54 Chevy shortbed is fresh out of the paint shop and we’ve been given the task of putting on the finishing pieces to the project. We lay in the wood bed, add some finishing components and this truck is ready for its first show.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - 2004 Corvette Gets Shocked
programme.desc: Kevin and Bryan upgrade the Federated Corvette with new suspension components.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - Hot Hybrid
programme.desc: High MPG doesn’t have to be boring. Kevin and Bryan add a few of their own touches to a new Prius.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - Car Collector Appreciation Day
programme.desc: Kevin heads out to California for a special car show celebrating the spirit of the car collector.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - 2004 Corvette Upgrades
programme.desc: Back to work giving our corvette a little extra horsepower.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - Air Flow System Diagnostics
programme.desc: With a rough running Impala in the shop, the guys check out the MAF sensor.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - Engine Cooling
programme.desc: Kevin and Bryan give a Saturn a once over before it gets donated to Wheels for Hope.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - 2004 Corvette Completion
programme.desc: Kevin and Bryan say goodbye to the Federated Corvette project and give a lucky Vette owner a new performance clutch.

programme.title: Two Guys Garage - 1967 Mustang Road Racer
programme.desc: A 67 Mustang Road Racer has a date with the Boss. A new Ford Racing “powerplant”.

programme.title: Truck U - EGR Cooling Delete
programme.desc: Why do head gaskets keep failing on Ford 6.0 Powerstroke Trucks? The EGR Cooler is starting to fail. Matt and Bruno will show you how to delete the problem for good on Truck U.

programme.title: Truck U - Blazer Trial
programme.desc: It’s the case of wreck the truck and pass the buck. A 1990 Blazer broke on the trail, but who’s at fault? Matt and Bruno gather evidence and make a final ruling on Truck U.

programme.title: Truck U - Medium Duty Maintenance
programme.desc: Big trucks need love to. So, we turn our attention to a GMC 5500 that needs maintenance before its next trip.

programme.title: Truck U - Can you Handle This?
programme.desc: Bruno and Matt see what they can do to improve the handling and sway on a 2001 GMC. Plus, they setup a very Cool Ram Runner Project. You won’t want to miss it!

programme.title: Latest News & Updates
programme.desc: Latest News & Action from the MotorRacing Channel.

programme.title: That Ski-Doo Feeling
programme.desc: Ski-Doo pilots take you on breathtaking adventures. This week it's off to Chile, Alaska and British Columbia.

programme.title: Searching for a jaguar in the jungle.
programme.desc: Photographer Richard Martins heads into the deep jungle with the hope of finding a jaguar.

programme.title: Can-Am Off Road Adventures
programme.desc: Get ready for the ultimate off-road showdown! In this action-packed series, watch Cowboy Cerrone, Travis Pastrana, Lia Block, Lucy Block, Hailie Deegan, Terry Madden, Kolt Gene, and Dale Brisby go head-to-head in a fierce competition for the coveted Great Off-Road Showdown championship belt.

programme.title: Wheels Through Time
programme.desc: Matt Walksler finds the bike of his dreams, a 1947 Harley-Davidson WL, rust and all.

programme.title: Utah Backcountry Riding

programme.desc: As outsiders to the Best In the Desert race series. The guys at S3 racing travel from Louisiana to compete vs. the best desert racers in the country, with their fully in house fabricated UTVs.

programme.title: Can-AM Presents: Visions of Victory
programme.desc: On this episode the S3 racing team heads to Las Vegas for the "Great American Off-Road Race". The prestigious Mint 400. Then the S3 Powersports takes on the Best in the Desert Silver State 300

programme.title: The Motocross Files
programme.desc: "The “O-Show” as he was called, Johnny O’Mara was part of the historic 1981 Motocross des Nations team and went on to become a 2-time National Champion on the powerful Honda team in the 1980’s. His ride at the 1986 Motocross des Nations event in Italy is perhaps one of the best ever.
