programme.title: Blair Wiggins Outdoors
programme.desc: Capt. Blair heads out to the very first spot we ever filmed a BWO show, with the first guide ever on BWO Capt. John Beach. This time they're catching sheepshead, hopefully nobody gets bit this time.

programme.title: We Caught That
programme.desc: Capt. Jason Dozier and Janot take a break from the guided trips, and take themselves on a hunting adventure.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: Season 11 is here and we’re kicking things off where every Florida Keys adventure begins: Key Largo. In this premiere episode, Rush Maltz jumps aboard a skiff with local captain Jake Leon to explore the shallow, clear waters that make Key Largo one of the most iconic fisheries in the Florida Keys. From stealthy presentations to explosive eats, this is inshore fishing at its finest. With unmatched access to the Atlantic Ocean, Florida Bay, and Everglades National Park, Key Largo truly lives up to its reputation as the gateway to paradise. From snook and sea trout to elusive bonefish, this episode sets the tone for a season built around world-class fishing, local eats, and unforgettable destinations. Get ready for the first stop of your Florida Keys experience.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: The next stop on our Florida Keys adventure brings Rush Maltz to the legendary waters of Islamorada — a destination known for its laid-back island vibe, world-class fishing, fine dining, and easy access to the reef. Joining locals Jimmy Hendrix and Jordi Hortensi, the game plan is simple: check in by boat, chase fish, eat well, and go dive. From kite fishing the reef to fresh seafood and great company, this episode is all about experiencing the true rhythm of a Florida Keys vacation.

programme.title: Making Waves in The Palm Beaches
programme.desc: Featuring the Chase ‘n Tailz Fishing Tournament, the North Palm Beach Rowing Club, the Palm Beach Sailing Club, holiday boat parade & the Palm Beach International Boat Show.

programme.title: Wild Palm Beaches
programme.desc: Featuring Mang Gear in Jupiter, PBC ERM snorkeling Peanut Island, Limestone Creek Urban Natural Area & the art of nature segment with Sheri Gansz.

programme.title: Reel Animals
programme.desc: Young Captain Reed Tudor hosts Captain Mike in Duck Key, FL and puts him on Micro Tarpon, Redfish and Goliath Grouper.

programme.title: Reel Animals
programme.desc: Captain Mike travels to the "Forgotten Coast" to fish Keaton Beach for the first time with the legendary Captain Greg DeVault.

programme.title: Captain's Log
programme.desc: Capt. Jonathan Moss is in Flamingo, Everglades National Park, in search of silver and gold.

programme.title: Flats Class
programme.desc: Captain C.A. heads to Brownsville, Texas, to chase trout and redfish with well-known local guide Captain Ernest Cisneros.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: Water quality is everything—and the Star brite - SeaSafe team is back at it for the second year in a row! Led by Cory Redwine, Conservation Director of Project SeaSafe, this is the 4th Annual Clean Water Collective and it’s bigger than ever. This time, we’re in Grant, Florida, where 4 million clams are being released into the Indian River Lagoon, paired with the planting of thousands of mangroves, oyster work by CCA and trash clean ups to help restore one of Florida’s most critical ecosystems. But it’s not just about hard work—it’s about connection. With local captains, fishing legends, and the Local Knowledge crew on hand, we hit the water to show why this place is worth fighting for. From clam drops by drone, to in-water mangrove planting, to monster snook catches, this is hands-on conservation at its best. This two-part series kicks off with action, heart, and the kind of hope only a healthy waterway can bring. Don’t miss Part 1—where conservation meets community and everyone gets their hands wet.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: As part of this year's Star brite Sea Safe event, we hit the water to experience some of the incredible fishing the Indian River Lagoon has to offer. For Rush, that meant some serious bow time with close friend Alex Hughey - on the hunt for some of the biggest snook we've ever encountered. The bite wasn't easy, but that didn't stop Rush from smashing his personal best - not once, but twice - in one unforgettable day on the water. Don't miss this action-packed episode of the Sea Safe adventure from beautiful Sebastian, Florida!

programme.title: Sail Kings
programme.desc: In the Florida Keys, legendary Capt. Paul Ross, son Capt. Pauly, and first mate Mason team up with Capt. Jared Raskob, where one Grouper drop leads to the unexpected.

programme.title: Captain's Log
programme.desc: With only a limited amount of time, Capt. Jonathan Moss is in Pine Island Sound, on the hunt for monster migratory Tarpon.

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger take Plantation Boat Mart’s new 36’ Invincible out to the swordfish grounds.

programme.title: The Direction
programme.desc: This week, we reflect on a tragic and sad memory in our lives as we remember a dear childhood friend of my son Nick, Nathan Wimsatt. Our families have been close for man years and shared a lot of time together around the soccer field, vacations, fishing trips, and much more. Nathan passed in a tragic car accident at only 18 years on April 29th, 2023. This week his Dad Brian is joining me on Lake Taylorsville where we'll share some memories and celebrate Nathan's life...that ended way to soon!

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: Join Rush and his good friend Anthony Solmo for an epic day of fishing in the stunning waters of Key West, Florida! Leaving the Odyssea at the dock, they hop on a bay boat outfitted with a trolling motor for the ultimate inshore adventure. Gliding quietly through the shallows, they target a variety of species—including hard-pulling Tarpon and explosive topwater Jacks—leaving Rush thinking, “A guy could get used to this kind of fishing!”

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: SoCal summer is calling, and the Local Knowledge crew is back on Ali’s home turf in San Diego, California. With reports of hot bites and fish moving in, the crew faces a classic Southern California dilemma — chase big yellowtail around the Coronado Islands or head north in search of bluefin tuna. The options are plenty, the challenges are real, and that’s exactly what keeps fishing in SoCal so exciting.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: When the yellowtail at the islands refuse to play, it’s time to switch up the game plan. Ali and Rush pivot north of San Diego in search of hard-charging bluefin tuna during a multi-day mission aboard the 43’ custom ACI Catamaran "Killer Bee". Armed with the right crew, the right gear, and a never-quit mindset, the guys roll the dice on an overnight move—hoping to wake up to redemption and put a few quality bluefin on deck.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: Returning to Venice, Louisiana for the first time in nearly a decade, Rush reconnects with longtime friends Eric and Monique Newman of Journey South Outfitters for an unforgettable inshore fishing adventure in the heart of the Mississippi River Delta. With reports of massive schools of redfish cruising the beaches, Monique (“Moe”) must decide whether to trust the intel and make a bold run down the coast—or rely on her own instincts built from years on the water. The mission is simple: put Rush on a true bull redfish and see if he has what it takes to earn a spot in her coveted “Over 40-Inch Club.” This trip highlights why Venice, Louisiana is considered one of the premier inshore fisheries in the country—and why having the right guide makes all the difference. Target Species: Bull Redfish. Location: Venice, Louisiana – Mississippi Delta. Outfitter: Journey South Outfitters.

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: In this episode, Slade ends the saga with a deer he has watched for a few years, Pope. On a previous episode you saw Slade put in all of the work with feeder and food plot strategy to make the stars align. Make sure you watch that episode as well to see the behind the scenes work it took to bring Pope to 25 yards.

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: In this episode, Slade and the new producer (Tim Guidroz) go out on their first turkey hunt together, and even though the weather is not ideal, they get the job done on a Wilkinson County gobbler. We also get a look at what the United Country - Southern States Realty office looks like during turkey season, with interviews from Scott Lindsey and Caleb Armstrong, and three big properties closing in Baton Rouge.

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: Season 4 of Hunting Land Man is starting off with a bang. In this episode, it’s opening week of turkey season in Mississippi, and these birds are doing it right. Cool mornings, gobbles breaking daylight, and longbeards fired up and on the move. We are fired up for the 2026 turkey season.

programme.title: Hunting Land Man
programme.desc: In this episode Slade gets very busy in the real estate world. But lucky for him, Caleb Armstrong takes up the slack. Caleb sets out to hunt swamp bucks on the public land of Tensas Parish.

programme.title: Captain's Log
programme.desc: Capt. Jonathan Moss is in Crystal River, Fl, taking the Cayo out into the Gulf in search of Shallow Water Grouper.

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger chase the full moon Wahoo bite fishing out of Plantation Boat Mart.

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: Captain Scott Walker heads swordfishing out of Hawks Cay with his friend, retired WWE wrestler Travis Tomko.

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: Captains Scott Walker and Ariel Medero fish with David Moss from the Nature Conservancy out of Hawks Cay sampling the bottom fishing and deep dropping. In between the action they learn about the Nature Conservancy’s mission to promote sustainable and transparent fishing practices.

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger break in Plantation Boat Mart’s new 36’ Invincible with some muttons for dinner and an epic shallow water sailfish double.

programme.title: Into The Blue
programme.desc: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger fish with Daiwa’s Marc Mills out of Plantation Boat Mart trying out all of Daiwa’s new jigging equipment.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Charles Rodney’s relationship with his hunting dogs, passion for field-to-table living and connection to the land make his love for chasing rabbits contagious while the heritage of Winchester’s small game ammunition makes this beloved pastime accessible for generations to come. Also: a Model 42 collection for the ages and hunting Kansas coyotes with the Keefer Brothers.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Deer season — the sights, the sounds and the smells stick with you. It’s about remembering those who came before us and setting the right example for those who look up to us. It’s about protecting the tradition, so they always have something to look forward to. When communities come together, we see the real reason for this special time of year.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc:
The team at Winchester Ammunition has arrived in Oregon to visit the Leupold Long Range Academy. There, they’ll put Winchester’s new “Supreme Long Range” ammunition to the test, pushing its performance across demanding long-range scenarios.
With precision, consistency, and confidence on the line, the team is eager to demonstrate exactly what “Precision Rifle Perfected” means when every shot counts.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Over a decade after the game changing Longbeard XR was introduced by Winchester, the wait is finally over for their next ground breaking turkey load. After years of perfecting the recipe behind the scenes, Winchester finally unveiled the new Longbeard TSS at a turkey camp in northern Michigan. First watch the jaw dropping Longbeard TSS pattern board testing, then see it go to work on some spring gobblers.

programme.title: Sail Kings
programme.desc: Featuring Capt. John Hagen & Capt. James Hagen in search of Sailfish in Islamorada.

programme.title: Sail Kings
programme.desc: Featuring Capt. Sammy Millazo Fishing Offshore Islamorada.

programme.title: Sail Kings
programme.desc: Featuring Capt. Charlie Scoble & Donnie Lang on a mother ship trip aboard the “TACKLE CENTER” to the Dry Tortugas Part 2.

programme.title: Sail Kings
programme.desc: Featuring Capt. Jon Cooper & Joe Neber Sail Fishing and Spearfishing in the beautiful waters of Costa Rica.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: The positive impact made by the shooting sports and recreational shooting to people across our country can’t be measured in just one story, so we have three. In Florida, Olympian Dania Vizzi trains at her home shooting facility and talk about how she went from ballerina to one of the best shotgun shooters in the world. In Nebraska, firearms instructor Thea Van Syoc has used the disciplines of competitive pistol shooting and training to help shape her life. Finally we’ll take a look into the National African American Gun Association and learn how one day at the range for Phillip Smith created the fastest growing firearm organization in the United States.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Nikki Boxler’s life in Update New York on her family farm raising cattle and producing maple syrup is a few thousand miles from the hills of New Mexico. We’ll take a look into Nikki’s life back home and then follow along as she visits the small town of Quemado and its bustling population of 228, while she pursues bugling elk with her favorite Winchester in hand. We’ll also head to Missouri and Texas to chase whitetails with Chris Keefer and Paul Sawyer.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: We set out to tell the tale of Marion - and his legendary Mack’s Prairie Wings, smack dab in the middle of the world famous Stuttgart, Arkansas - the land of ducks and rice. Get an inside look at a man that many consider to be larger than life, from those that knew him best, like his cousin and fellow hell raiser, Bud McCollum. We’ll even hear a story or two from Milton Stovesand, an 81 year old former waterfowl guide and Mack’s employee who actually guided John Olin a time or two, and ran the timber holes with Marion. It’s timber holes and tall tales. It’s cups of coffee and cupped up wings. It’s partners in business, and partners in potholes and it’s pure Arkansas...just like Marion.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: Melissa Bachman has made her name over the last two decades as one of the most dedicated hunters and producers in the outdoor industry. In this episode we’ll tag along with Melissa while she chases big game around the world with her Winchester in hand. First we’ll head to Colorado as Melissa spot and stalks giant mule deer, then we’ll head across the pond to New Zealand to chase mountain ibex and tar, then head back to the states for Montana whitetail at ground level.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: In this episode Andy and his friend Scotty take on Kodiak Island, Alaska for an adventure of Salmon fishing and hunting black tail deer. Whilst traversing steep mountains in the company of Kodiak brown bears and taking on cold Alaskan waters, Andy presents useful information for fellow outdoor enthusiasts all whilst building friendships with the great people of Kodiak Big Timber Lodge.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: In this episode Andy goes back to his home farm in Alabama for deer and quail hunting. Not only does he make incredible dishes with his former ‘masterchef’ contestant Alex which he serves to local children in need but he explains how hunting is never about “just the hunt” but about the camaraderie, friendship and memories made through the pursuit of the harvest.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: In part 1 of the British episodes, Andy starts his road trip around the United Kingdom in the Cotswolds where he sets out to hunt and taste the beautiful venison that Michelin star chef, Mark Robinson has to offer. He then proceeds to travel to the border of Scotland where he samples some of the finest and most historic bird shooting practices found throughout the British Isles.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: In part 2 of the British episodes, Andy visits the majestic land of Scotland. Here he is introduced to Scottish deer stalking, a type of hunting that is full of history and etiquette. He hunts ‘monarch’ stags and goes into the Atlantic waters to find some of the freshest shellfish on the planet which he, and Mike Robinson cook and eat. Concluding the British episodes in a place as wild as Scotland really sets the tone for the series.

programme.title: Local Lines
programme.desc: In this episode Captain Brandon Branch decided to go back to his roots and fish with a long time friend Laremy Strickland. They've been fishing together for over 10 years now and on this episode they'll be competing to see who can catch the biggest bag.

programme.desc: Captain Brand Branch ends season one of Local Lines with a bang as he fishes with his good friend and Cedar Key Legend Captain Danny Allen of Southern Salt Charters. This may be the last episode of the season but it's just the beginning of an amazing journey.

Another interesting tidbit is that Mark was actually on Sam Rayburn fishing when the space shuttle blew up; he actually heard the explosion and felt the rumble.

programme.desc: John and a group of friends make an annual winter trip to catch smallmouth in the dead of KY wintertime on Dale Hollow Lake. Dale Hollow holds the world record smallmouth catch. programme.title: Rivers are Life
programme.desc: One of the most iconic rivers in the world, the Nile has been fished for thousands of years, but in recent decades fishing has nearly halted in many areas due to plastic clogging the river. VeryNile launched the Reviving Cairo Fishermen project, which empowers local fishermen and women to clean the Nile, removing almost 40 tons of plastic per year. The plastic they collect is then turned into sellable products, completing a circular economy that benefits the entire community.

programme.title: Rivers are Life
programme.desc: At age seven, Cash “The Conservation Kid” Daniels began leading cleanups along the Tennessee River in his hometown, Chattanooga. Six years later, Cash continues to make a significant impact on the river’s health by strategically collecting fishing waste and inspiring his peers across the world to protect the environment and all the wildlife he holds dear.

programme.title: Operation Healing Heroes
programme.desc: Shot through the neck on Fort Hood in Texas, Green Beret John Arroyo recounts coming face to face with his attacker and Jesus blinding his would be killer.

programme.title: Wildbound by Forloh
programme.desc: In this episode Andy goes back to his home farm in Alabama for deer and quail hunting. Not only does he make incredible dishes with his former ‘masterchef’ contestant Alex which he serves to local children in need but he explains how hunting is never about “just the hunt” but about the camaraderie, friendship and memories made through the pursuit of the harvest.

programme.title: Reel Animals
programme.desc: Mike fished for shallow water Grouper with Capt. Brandon Branch and Mike Bedenbaugh.

programme.title: Reel Animals
programme.desc: Mike Fishes a very special Tarpon tournament out of South Seas Island Resort.

programme.title: Reel Animals
programme.desc: Mike Fishes with long time friend Erick Bachnik, from MirrOlure, deep inside the Manatee River for Giant Snook.

programme.title: Reel Animals
programme.desc: Join Mike on this special adventure into some of the best locations Citrus County has to offer.

programme.title: Blair Wiggins Outdoors
programme.desc: Coastal Conservation Association Florida Star Tournament redfish releases.

programme.title: Blair Wiggins Outdoors
programme.desc: Capt. Blair heads down to Islamorada to get some deep water groceries with the Starbrite team.

programme.title: Blair Wiggins Outdoors
programme.desc: When the captain calls and says the weather in Alaska is great, you don't waste the opportunity. So Capt. Blair and the whole crew head out for a day of fishing. With one camera on the boat, things got a little chaotic with all the catching going on.

programme.title: Blair Wiggins Outdoors
programme.desc: Blair is flats fishing off the west coast of Florida with Capt. Greg DeVault.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: It might be cold and windy, but if you have trust in your guide the result could be an epic day of fishing! Rush steps up to the challenge and puts Ali on an incredible bite of mutton snapper while also checking another species off of Ali's bucket list!

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: As season 8 comes to a close, Ali and Rush reflect on some of the more memorable moments on the water. From the Florida Keys to San Diego, Guatemala, and the Bahamas, the show has taken us on quite the adventures with more to come!

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: The boys are back in the Florida Keys to kick off the new season. Ali gets to check out Rush's new digs at the Ocean's Edge Resort and take a ride on the new, Mercury powered SeaVee 34Oz. Rush has been dialing in his new live bait set up and has a few freshies to hopefully feed to a hungry wahoo. And if that doesn't work, there's always something great to catch in the Florida Keys.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: Ali and Rush head to Everglades City Florida to meet up with local Capt. Jimmy Wheeler of the Everglades Fishing Company to get a taste of what the saying "Old Florida" really means. With the SeaVee 270Z bay boat in tow, the guys look to Jimmy to put them on the bite, fishing the shallow inshore wrecks and rock piles for Snook, Grouper, and Cobia.

programme.title: Operation Healing Heroes
programme.desc: An Honor No One Wants: A Gold Star Family is the immediate family member(s) of a fallen soldier who died while serving in a time of conflict. Sheila Mitchell-Murphy honors the memory her Son…American hero Etienne Murphy.

programme.title: Operation Healing Heroes
programme.desc: 82 year old Navy veteran with more than 2.7 million TikTok followers, Patriotic Kenny talks of how he helps other vets through him gifting mobility scooters to fellow veterans in need.

programme.title: Operation Healing Heroes
programme.desc: U.S. Army veteran Domingo Martinez recounts overcoming the life altering effects of trauma he received as a child and on the DMZ.

programme.title: Operation Healing Heroes
programme.desc: U.S. Coast Guard helicopter pilot Shay Williams recounts his career flying countless search and rescue missions.

programme.title: Hard Truths of Conservation
programme.desc: Dan Cabela sets out to pursue, capture and aid in the conservation of the tallest land mammal in North America – the Moose.

programme.title: Hard Truths of Conservation
programme.desc: Dan Cabela explores the challenges and research behind living with Africa’s most secretive predator - the Leopard.

programme.title: Hard Truths of Conservation
programme.desc: Dan Cabela explores the model for community conservation in the Zambezi Delta of Mozambique, and responsibly harvests a Cape Buffalo bull on their behalf.

programme.title: Hard Truths of Conservation
programme.desc: Dan Cabela learns about the recovery of an icon and the role that utilization has played in bringing a species back from the brink - the American Bison.

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: With Meredith McCord halfway around the world, Capt. Justin Leake gives show producer Chris Hermans his first shot at landing a tarpon after filming them for 9 seasons. Does art imitate life?

programme.title: Chasin' The Sun
programme.desc: Capt. Justin Leake joins some old friends on board the Fall Line in hopes of scoring big in the Bay Point Billfish Open. This trip will test the limits of all the anglers as the skilled captain and crew do the impossible in hopes of bringing home the prize.

programme.title: Breaking Boundaries
programme.desc: Bound for Lake Ontario, JP is facing tough weather conditions when trying to target smallmouth bass in the early season.

programme.title: Baitfuel Fishing Tournament
programme.desc: The final qualifier for the season finds the teams in Cardinal, Ontario, taking on the St. Lawrence River. It’s the last chance for teams to qualify for the Tour Championship.

programme.title: Captain's Log
programme.desc: Capt. Jonathan Moss ventures to South Florida with the hope of having a few Exotic Encounters.

programme.title: Captain's Log
programme.desc: Despite adverse conditions, Capt. Jonathan Moss and long time friend, Capt. Adam Whitson, are heading out on the water in search of the Crystal River Inshore Slam.

programme.title: Captain's Log
programme.desc: Capt. Jonathan Moss is heading offshore into the Gulf of America, eager to feed his offshore fishing obsession.

programme.title: Captain's Log
programme.desc: Central Florida’s St. John’s River is home to countless species of wildlife, making this sweet water estuary the perfect playground for a pair of river rats, Captains Jonathan Moss and Nick Clark.

programme.title: Dirt
programme.desc: Episode one hits close to home. Specifically, Josh’s hometown of Seattle where we travel from the mountains to the coast in pursuit of local ingredients for an authentic Washington meal. Digging for forest delicacies, hunting for exotic bivalves, and exploring the tidal flats of a multi-generational shellfish farm—it’s all on the menu.

programme.title: Dirt
programme.desc: In Episode 2: New Orleans, Josh links up with Mason Hereford (owner of award-winning sandwich shop Turkey and the Wolf) and then hits the road for some epic hog hunting, swamp fishing, crawfish and oyster harvesting, and exploring Louisiana’s only water buffalo dairy farm. It all culminates in one incredible feast featuring unique regional dishes prepared by Mason with the help of some local friends.

programme.title: Saltwater Experience
programme.desc: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor use Lowrance Active Target all day to find fish, first targeting bridge tarpon then finding a variety of species in Florida Bay mullet muds.

programme.title: Saltwater Experience
programme.desc: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor chase Tarpon of all sizes around Florida Bay and the Everglades.

programme.title: Saltwater Experience
programme.desc: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor catch spotted sea trout and jacks on the grass flats and potholes in Florida Bay.

programme.title: Saltwater Experience
programme.desc: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor head to the iconic Seven Mile Bridge targeting big jack crevasses and permit.

programme.title: Saltwater Experience
programme.desc: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor head offshore to the Marathon Hump, taking advantage of the 26-feet hybrid boat's versatility to find some exciting Blackfin and Skipjack Tuna fishing.

programme.title: Saltwater Experience
programme.desc: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by scientist Nick Haddad, who educates our hosts and the fishing world on the various descending devices used to preserve stressed catch from deeper waters as the three of them get into some fun Red Snapper fishing off the coast of Marathon, Florida in the Keys.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: Trenton meets up with Tom Brewbaker as they fly down to Venice Louisiana to fish with Mike Frenette. Mike runs the Redfish Lodge of Louisiana and has over forty years of angling experience. Mike teaches the guys about the local waters including pogey boats and most importantly, world class red fish.

programme.title: Local Knowledge
programme.desc: Returning to Venice, Louisiana for the first time in nearly a decade, Rush reconnects with longtime friends Eric and Monique Newman of Journey South Outfitters for an unforgettable inshore fishing adventure in the heart of the Mississippi River Delta. With reports of massive schools of redfish cruising the beaches, Monique (“Moe”) must decide whether to trust the intel and make a bold run down the coast—or rely on her own instincts built from years on the water. The mission is simple: put Rush on a true bull redfish and see if he has what it takes to earn a spot in her coveted “Over 40-Inch Club.” This trip highlights why Venice, Louisiana is considered one of the premier inshore fisheries in the country—and why having the right guide makes all the difference. Target Species: Bull Redfish. Location: Venice, Louisiana – Mississippi Delta. Outfitter: Journey South Outfitters.

programme.title: Adventure Cities
programme.desc: Seasoned travel writer Jonathan Thompson journeys to Sydney, Australia, in search of the most unforgettable adventure experiences this iconic city has to offer.

programme.title: Destination Dive
programme.desc: We will take you on an incredible journey visiting the most iconic wrecks and reefs in this area of the Keys. And we will also explore the groundbreaking work that scientists and volunteers are working on to shade corals from the harsh sunlight and extreme ocean temperatures.

programme.title: Dirt
programme.desc: In our final episode in Japan, the DIRT team went road-tripping through beautiful, snowy Japanese landscapes—and tasting everything in sight. A crown jewel in the northeast corner of Japan, Hokkaido may be famous among skiers and snowboarders for the luscious "Japow," but way beyond the tourist trail there's a burgeoning new food scene and a unique and vibrant history. Though, if we're honest, Josh probably could've spent the whole trip carving up the mountain and been a happy camper.

programme.title: Hard Truths of Conservation
programme.desc: Big horn sheep are one of North America’s most spectacular animals, but future conservation requires large-scale, hands-on efforts. Joining a multi-agency translocation in Texas, Dan Cabela is in the thick of it.

programme.title: The Green Way Outdoors
programme.desc: Kyle takes to the water in a way few anglers dare, fishing from a Sea-Doo in search of the ancient lake sturgeon, a species that’s been around since the age of the dinosaurs and can live over 100 years. Hooking into one of North America’s largest freshwater fish from a personal watercraft proves to be a challenge unlike any other.

programme.title: Fox Hollow Outdoors
programme.desc: Michael and Larken travel to South Carolina's Lowcountry for a massive barbecue charity event supporting children battling serious illness. After serving up smoke for a great cause, they spend an extra day in the marsh chasing redfish and speckled trout.

programme.title: Stomping Grounds
programme.desc: Host Ryan McVinney takes Boat Trader fans on an indepth exploration of Florida flats fishing deep in the backcountry of Islamorada. Featuring Captain Eric Lund, a Wall Street Broker turned backcountry fishing guide, this episode reveals the key design features of the world’s best flats skiffs and how they are built for targeting some of the most exciting sportfish species on the planet. Also, covered are bay boats and their versatility for families exploring Florida’s coastal waterways, along with a detailed study of fish species and wildlife in the region. Some of the area’s most beautiful and secret locales show why Islamorada has earned the nickname of the Sportfish Capital of The World.

programme.title: The Cast
programme.desc: Trenton makes a trip to South Carolina to fish Lake Murray. He meets up with the Rather Outdoors crew to spend time on the water and at the Rather HQ checking out all the gear from Lews and Strike King.

programme.title: Saltwater Experience
programme.desc: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor chase Tarpon of all sizes around Florida Bay and the Everglades.

programme.desc: Passionate about hunting and fishing, Michel Bélisle, Éric Lacelle and brothers Éric and Martin Marion invite us into their world.

programme.title: Liquid Edge Ep104
programme.desc: Liquid Edge is surfing barrels on Hawaii’s North Shore, making friends with sharks, enjoying epic waterfalls in Mexico, traveling to Indonesia with professional surfers, hanging ten with one of longboarding’s beauties...and it’s not surfing if you aren’t getting slammed, plus plenty more on Liquid Edge.

programme.title: Sportsman's Adventures: Fishing With a Legend in Costa Rica
programme.desc: Captain Rick Murphy and Captain Ridge Murphy take to the Fish Aggregating Devices of Quepos, Costa Rica thanks to Casa Vieja Lodge on The Hooker.

programme.title: Facing Waves Ep808: Canoe Tripping in Killarney Provincial Park – Ontario, Canada
programme.desc: Facing Waves host Ken Whiting embarks on an epic, 5-day backcountry canoe adventure in Killarney Provincial Park in Ontario, Canada.

programme.title: World of Watersports Ep317: Whitewater Kayaking - Arctic Circle, Norway
programme.desc: German extreme kayaking superstar Adrian Mattern sets out together with a team of four to explore the whitewater treasures of Northern Norway at the Arctic Circle.

programme.title: Let's Take It Outside Ep101: Where Adventure Comes Naturally – Niagara Falls, USA
programme.desc: Join Misty on an historic journey through Niagara Falls, from braving the river to catch Walleye, Trout and Salmon to cruising on the “Maid of the Mist”, Niagara Falls... Where Adventure comes Naturally.

programme.title: World of Watersports Ep321: Cliff Diver Rhiannan Iffland + The Aegean 600 Offshore Yacht Race – Greece
programme.desc: A profile of world-class cliff diver Rhiannan Iffland, plus the challenging Aegean 600 Offshore Yacht Race in Greece.

programme.title: Shark Dive TV Ep108: Hammerhead Sharks + Blue Hole Adventure in Belize
programme.desc: Shark Dive TV explores the odd behavior of Hammerhead Sharks; goes on a Blue Hole adventure in Belize featuring a friendly shark; and takes a Deeper Dive to study Scalloped Hammerhead Sharks and Nurse Sharks.

programme.title: Liquid Edge Ep103
programme.desc: One of the biggest surf days ever at Teahpoo in Tahiti. Interview with Surfing Hall of Famer Brock Little. Filmmaker Josh Pomer gives us a closer look at the infamous wave at Mavericks. We catch big fish from a kayak with big game kayak angler, Jim Salmons, and hit Maui’s windsurfing hot spots with Robbie Naish.

programme.title: Blue World Ep801: Zach Gets Certified + Filming Goliaths
programme.desc: In the Season 8 premiere, Jonathan’s longtime cameraman/editor decides to break out of his desk job and get scuba certified. Then they travel to Florida to film goliath groupers—huge fish that reach 800 pounds!

programme.title: Facing Waves Ep605: Paddling Oregon’s Rugged Coast – USA
programme.desc: Facing Waves explores the rugged Oregon Coast by sea kayak, SUP and whitewater kayak.

programme.title: Nomads Seeking Adventure Ep103: Whitewater Kayaking in New Zealand
programme.desc: On the other side of the World New Zealand is a small country with big possibilities. An international team kayaks the classic runs of New Zealand’s South Island…experiencing a new adventure around every bend.

programme.title: Xplash Ep105
programme.desc: Xplash brings you eye-popping water-related action from around the globe. In this episode, we’ll visit Bandon, Oregon to check out the creation of a sandy labyrinth; ride on the Billabong Clipper to surf remote locations around the globe; and get a taste of the multi-sport party known as the Swatch Free-4-Style.

programme.title: Water Matters Ep101 - Freshwater
programme.desc: Water Matters documents water-related environmental issues from around the globe as a number of the world’s best documentary filmmakers and environmental organizations share their stories regarding the planet’s water crisis as well as potential solutions for a healthier world.

programme.title: Hot Water Ep107: Jet Sprinting V8 Superboat Championships from Cabarita Beach – New South Wales, Australia
programme.desc: Jet Sprinting from Cabarita Beach in New South Wales, Australia. V8 Unlimited Superboats are the top racing class in Jet Sprinting with the boats being unrestricted.

programme.title: Blue World Ep803: Atlantic Wolffish in Maine, USA + Cozumel’s Aerolito Cave
programme.desc: Blue World heads to northern Maine to meet a famous Atlantic wolffish couple named Bob and Jane, and learn how they catch crabs for dinner. Then Blue World travels to Cozumel to explore a cave containing some of the most unique and rare cave fauna in the world.

programme.title: World of Watersports Ep206: Poseidon Games…Hydroflight – Australia + World Kiteboarding Championships
programme.desc: Australia’s Gold Coast is home to the Poseidon Games where high-tech has birthed a new watersport called Hydroflight; then we recap the full season of the World Kiteboarding Championship Tour.

programme.title: Facing Waves Ep104: Stand-Up Paddling Puerto Rico
programme.desc: Explore the magical stand-up paddling opportunities that Puerto Rico has to offer…from a competitive race, to epic surf breaks.

programme.title: Facing Waves Ep208: Kayak Fishing & Stand-Up Paddling in Puerto Rico
programme.desc: Join stand-up paddling legend Mike Simpson on his circumnavigation of Puerto Rico, and experience catching 150lb tarpon from a kayak.

programme.title: Sportsman's Adventures: Searching for Sharks off Key Largo, Florida
programme.desc: Captain Rick Murphy and Captain Ridge Murphy search for sharks off Key Largo, Florida.

programme.title: Next Stop Ep114: Newport, Oregon – USA
programme.desc: Next Stop checks out the Oregon Coast Aquarium, Newport Celtic Festival and Highland Games, Marine Discovery Tours, Newport Farmers Market, Mos Clam Chowder and the Mistral Art Nanas Irish Pub.

programme.title: Water Ways TV Ep206: An Epic Journey…Toronto to Montreal – Canada
programme.desc: Water Ways TV host Steven Bull embarks on an epic journey with his “starter boat” traveling from Toronto to Montreal.

programme.title: World of Watersports Ep326: Cliff Diving in India + History of Stand-Up Paddleboarding
programme.desc: World Champion Rhiannan Iffland and cliff diving legend Orlando Duque put on a Cliff Diving Exhibition in India; then Tristan Boxford gives us an overview of the history of the exciting new watersport...Stand-Up Paddleboarding.

programme.title: Let's Take It Outside Ep102: The Happiest Country on the Planet – Costa Rica (Part 1)
programme.desc: Costa Rica is coined the happiest country on the planet; it’s a place where your fishing dreams often can come true. In this episode, Misty is targeting her ‘bucket list’ fish Roosterfish, and you won’t believe their rare Whale experience.

programme.title: Shark Dive TV Ep110: Bull Sharks in Cancun, Mexico + Sharks Electrosensory Systems
programme.desc: Shark Dive TV dives with Bull Sharks in Cancun, Mexico and analyzes shark's electrosensory systems.

programme.title: Nomads Seeking Adventure Ep113: Breaking Barriers - Kayaking China (Part 1)
programme.desc: In this first part of a two part journey, American adventurers embark on a trip of a lifetime to explore the uncharted watershed of China's Sichuan province and to experience the different cultures China has to offer.

programme.title: Nomads Seeking Adventure Ep114: Breaking Barriers - Kayaking China (Part 2)
programme.desc: Part two of the journey reveals another part of the reason the American adventurers came to China. To be ambassadors for the sport of kayaking.

programme.title: Hot Water Ep115: Aquabike World Championship Series - Qingdao, China (Part 2)
programme.desc: The UIM-ABP Haier Grand Prix continues in Qingdao, China, and features the Aquabike Freestyle and Runabout Divisions.

programme.title: Blue World Ep807: Cave Drift Dive + Triple Wreck Dive
programme.desc: Host Jonathan Bird dives a famous, and extremely dangerous, cave in Florida known as Friedman Sink, where divers exit at "Catfish Hotel". Then Blue World heads to the Florida coast where the team explores three shipwrecks on a single dive.

programme.title: Water Ways TV Ep207: Navigating Montreal’s historic Lachine Canal – Canada
programme.desc: Water Ways TV host Steven Bull works on some boat upgrades in Quebec before navigating Montreal’s historic Lachine Canal.

programme.title: Fly Rod Chronicles: Argentina Slam
programme.desc: Curtis and Autumn travel to Argentina and catch the Grand Slam of a lifetime!

programme.title: Airtime Ep41: Kayaking The Mighty Rivers of Africa
programme.desc: The world’s top kayakers travel Africa in search of the most dangerous whitewater. Shot over the course of a year, this episode highlights the travel and adventure of world-class kayakers as they embark on a “bigger and better” hunt for the largest waterfalls in a land where emergency services are virtually non-existent.

programme.title: Facing Waves Ep601: Paddling Wales – United Kingdom
programme.desc: The Facing Waves team heads to Wales to explore the coast via stand-up paddleboard; surf the legendary Snowdonia inland surf lagoon; and learn the sport of coasteering.

programme.title: Facing Waves Ep604: Paddling the Great Calusa Blueway – Florida, USA
programme.desc: Luke Hopkins and Anne Pagano explore one of the world’s great waterway trails via stand-up paddle board...Florida’s Great Calusa Blueway Paddling Trail.

programme.title: Let's Take It Outside Ep103: A Country Like No Other – Costa Rica (Part 2)
programme.desc: Costa Rica is a country like no other: Get ready to chase down Yellowfin Tuna in a pod of Dolphins; hike waterfalls; and trek through the rainforest in search of the perfect surfing beach.

programme.title: Xplash Ep106
programme.desc: Xplash brings you the coolest action in, on, and near water. Today we’ll get soaked in Africa's great Nile River while kayak surfing; Maya Gabeira goes in search of the biggest wave ever surfed by a woman; and our first stop takes us windsurfing in Maui.

programme.title: The Bassquatch Hunter: Kayak Fishing vs TV Sports Reporter
programme.desc: Mike takes Brooke Fletcher, of Bally Sports, out for her first time in a fishing kayak! The former Miss Georgia not only catches her first fish, but also had an amazing time doing it! After fishing in the remote quarries outside of Chicago, Brooke takes Mike into her studio and shows him what it is like to be on live TV every day!

programme.title: World of Watersports Ep243: Powerboat World Championships + New York Yacht Club Invitational + Kitefoil World Tour + Red Bull Sailing Team
programme.desc: Powerboat World Championships + New York Yacht Club Invitational + Kitefoil World Tour in Pingtan, China + Red Bull Sailing Team Profile

programme.title: Blue World Ep810: A Search for a Stargazer + Playful Seals
programme.desc: Host Jonathan Bird returns to one of his favorite dive sites, the Blue Heron Bridge in Riviera Beach, Florida, in search of a rare and amazing fish: The Stargazer. Then he invites his wife Christine and son Liam to meet some playful seals in Massachusetts.

programme.title: Nomads Seeking Adventure Ep205: South American Whitewater Kayak Adventure
programme.desc: A team of world-class kayakers heads south to the far reaches of Chile’s lush and unpopulated landscape in search of the most challenging whitewater on a kayak expedition like no other, filled with tight canyons, breathtaking waterfalls and non-stop action.

programme.title: Facing Waves Ep706: Stand-Up Paddling Montréal and Cantons-de-l’Est – Quebec, Canada
programme.desc: Luke Hopkins heads to Montréal, Québec, to put his stand-up paddling skills to the ultimate test on the huge waves of the Saint Lawrence River’s Lachine Rapids. Alex Kellar then explores the nearby tranquil rivers and lakes via stand-up paddleboard.

programme.title: Water Matters Ep103: Plastics & Pollution
programme.desc: Water Matters documents water-related environmental issues from around the globe as a number of the world’s best documentary filmmakers and environmental organizations share their stories regarding the planet’s water crisis as well as potential solutions for a healthier world.

programme.title: Hot Water Ep124: F1H2O Powerboat World Championship Tour from the United Arab Emirates
programme.desc: The UIM F1H2O Powerboat World Championship Tour heads to Sharjah in the United Arab Emirates for highlights from the sixth and final round of the F1H2O Powerboat World Championships.

programme.title: Water Ways TV Ep201: Vancouver Island – Canada
programme.desc: Water Ways TV host Steven Bull checks out Vancouver Island, from deep sea fishing to inland lakes with a special appearance by the Canadian Forces Snowbirds.

programme.title: Liquid Edge Ep104
programme.desc: Liquid Edge is surfing barrels on Hawaii’s North Shore, making friends with sharks, enjoying epic waterfalls in Mexico, traveling to Indonesia with professional surfers, hanging ten with one of longboarding’s beauties...and it’s not surfing if you aren’t getting slammed, plus plenty more on Liquid Edge.

programme.title: Sportsman's Adventures: Fishing With a Legend in Costa Rica
programme.desc: Captain Rick Murphy and Captain Ridge Murphy take to the Fish Aggregating Devices of Quepos, Costa Rica thanks to Casa Vieja Lodge on The Hooker.

programme.title: Facing Waves Ep808: Canoe Tripping in Killarney Provincial Park – Ontario, Canada
programme.desc: Facing Waves host Ken Whiting embarks on an epic, 5-day backcountry canoe adventure in Killarney Provincial Park in Ontario, Canada.

programme.title: World of Watersports Ep317: Whitewater Kayaking - Arctic Circle, Norway
programme.desc: German extreme kayaking superstar Adrian Mattern sets out together with a team of four to explore the whitewater treasures of Northern Norway at the Arctic Circle.

programme.title: Shark Dive TV Ep109: Whale Sharks, Feeding Frenzies, and Great White Sharks.
programme.desc: Shark Dive TV studies Whale Sharks kept in a giant tank and then takes a Deeper Dive by explaining shark hearing, shark feeding frenzies and Great White Sharks.

programme.title: Let’s Take It Outside Ep109: Old Florida with a New Vibe…Discovering West Volusia County, Florida
programme.desc: Do you wonder what “Old Florida” is like? Discover West Volusia County with Misty as she catches the biggest Bass of her life, kayaks crystal clear springs and races across the waters chasing gators.

programme.title: World of Watersports Ep313: Epic Journey Across Austrian & Italian Alps by Bike and Kayak
programme.desc: Extreme kayakers Adrian Mattern and Brendan Orton pursue an ambitious goal: a journey across the Austrian and Italian Alps only by bicycle and their kayaks. In addition, we look at the challenges and the technology involved in setting up a racecourse for watersports such as kiteboarding, sailing, and stand-up paddleboarding.

programme.title: The Blue Realm Ep113: Lions of the Deep
programme.desc: In the past 50 years, sea lion populations have declined more than 80% along the North Pacific coast. Scientists at the University of British Columbia, and the Vancouver Aquarium, are working together to help save North America’s iconic and largest pinniped - the stellar sea lion.

programme.title: Blue World Ep202: Manta Rays in Micronesia + Fish Rescue + Tiger Sharks in the Bahamas
programme.desc: Host Jonathan Bird travels to Micronesia to learn about Manta Rays, helps rescue tropical fish in New England and dives with Tiger Sharks in the Bahamas

programme.title: The Surfing Lifestyle Ep108: West Java in Indonesia
programme.desc: A group of Australian pro junior surfers jump aboard the ‘Just Dreaming’ Indonesian charter boat and unleash some of the most hi-fi action as they push the limits of where surfing is going. Hear their thoughts regarding the competitive life, where they hope to be and what it’s like to unwind at the end of the year, while still having the competitive fires burning as they search for perfect Indonesian waves.

programme.title: Travel Quest Ep108: North Pole
programme.desc: The Travel Quest crew takes an icebreaker to the North Pole just in time to make a satellite phone call to wish Bill's mother a happy birthday (she is so shocked she doesn't recognize him). We also track and film the biggest predators on earth…polar bears.

programme.title: Water Matters Ep102: Ocean Impact
programme.desc: Water Matters documents water-related environmental issues from around the globe as a number of the world’s best documentary filmmakers and environmental organizations share their stories regarding the planet’s water crisis as well as potential solutions for a healthier world.

programme.title: Hot Water Ep108: Behind-The-Scenes of Unlimited Superboat Jet Sprinting
programme.desc: Hot Water goes behind-the-scenes of Unlimited Superboat Jet Sprinting. V8 Unlimited Superboats are the top racing class in Jet Sprinting with the boats being unrestricted.

programme.title: Water Ways TV Ep211: Nova Scotia, Canada
programme.desc: Host Steven Bull explores the history Nova Scotia with a to-the-centimetre replica of the “Hector”, checks out the oldest Yacht Club in the Americas and then goes Tidal Bore Rafting.

programme.title: Next Stop Ep107: Maui, Hawaii - USA
programme.desc: Adventures on Maui include ATV riding at Kahomo Ranch, watching Cirque Polynesia, sailing on Teralani, enjoying the Wailele Polynesian Luau and having fun at Mulligans/Willie K.

programme.title: Shark Encounters in the Deep
programme.desc: Hand-feeding and “flipping” Tiger Sharks in the Bahamas, diving with Great Whites near Mexico’s Guadalupe Island, learn how to put sharks into a relaxed state known as “Tonic Immobility”, enabling sharks to serve as camera operators.

programme.title: Lost In Tasmania
programme.desc: Lost In Tasmania takes viewers on a journey of discovery and adventure in search of surfing nirvana - perfect waves! We travel with local surfer Mikey Brennan as he and friends explore the secluded East Coast of Tasmania, the Tasman Peninsula and famed Shipstern Bluff where they find incredible scenery and epic waves.

programme.title: Journeys In Africa Ep206: Victoria Falls…The Smoke That Thunders
programme.desc: One of the 7 Natural Wonders of the World, Victoria Falls is an amazing spectacle that straddles the Zambia/Zimbabwe border. The force of the water is so intense that the water bouncing back creates a rainforest. Remarkably, the wildlife in the area is as abundant as the water going over the falls.

programme.title: The Therapy Crouch - You May As Well Move In Then
programme.desc:
On this week’s episode, Pete provides a lesson on exactly how not to ask the love of your life to move in with you.
Learn more about Peter’s revolutionary invention, ‘The Squat Box’ and why you should never leave your home movie collection lying around when your future wife and mother-in-law are coming round for tea.
From Pet Detectives to interior design clashes, no stone is left unturned on The Therapy Crouch.

programme.title: The Therapy Crouch made some new friends....and they do not disappoint
programme.desc:
The moment you’ve all been waiting for has finally arrived! The Therapy Crouch has waited almost a year for this very special moment and boy was it worth it…
This week, we are joined by fellow podcasters and new best friends, James Buckley and Clair Buckley!
It’s fair to say that this is a bit of a milestone for the podcast and nothing was off the table in this no holds barred chat, from how to pity a girl into bed to James’ truly teeth woes.
They are also a welcome addition to Agony Ab couch, where we hear from a woman who is being constantly undermined in the workplace which Clair has some FANTASTIC advice for.
Do not miss this one!

programme.title: The Therapy Crouch’s Christmas Party!!!
programme.desc:
It is a double celebration in the Crouch household after Scott pulled his finger out and asked the big Q - which has prompted some discussions on Abbey trading hers in for an upgrade (ring not fella)
Although it isn’t all festive cheer as the pair have been up to their usual antics and have come locked and loaded with multiple weekly whines - a lot of of them regarding the other’s fashion faux pas.
And fear not, we are not abandoning you guys with your own relationship woes, Agony Ab is still on the couch to help with your long distance heart ache and those of you who are struggling with husbands who have little to no interest in putting the Christmas tree up!! Bah humbug!
Enjoy this week’s Therapy Crouch

programme.title: They Call Me The Godfather
programme.desc:
Welcome back to The Therapy Crouch! On this week’s episode Abbey and Peter are on hand to share some pearls of wisdom on what makes a good godparent, and some top tips on how to expertly navigate a thanks but no thanks conversation with your nearest and dearest.
In Agony Ab, Abbey and Peter are on hand to help when your better half suddenly decides to take up the gym 10 years too late and we hear from Louis, one of our international listeners who needs some advice on how to make the first move!
We hope you enjoy this very special episode from the OG Podfather and your fairy Podmother!

programme.title: To Dream The Impossible Dream
programme.desc:
In this week’s Therapy Crouch, Abbey and Peter take a while to sit down and discuss some of their dreams and aspirations both as a pair of youngsters and now as one of the UK’s most prominent power couples.
We hear about Abbey’s early days in Genie Queen - Liverpool’s answer to the Sugababes - and a young football protege with dreams of scoring 100 premier league goals and becoming the nation’s Podfather in the process.
We also hear from our lovely listeners with their Weekly Whines and Agony Abs. We hear about one of listeners whose girlfriend has being using their toothbrush for bit of DIY and a fellow NETTO alumni has been in touch with a playground anthem that has been sung for generations across Merseyside.
Enjoy this week’s Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Top O' the Mornin to Ya
programme.desc:
In honour of St Patrick, the Therapy Crouch goes international and takes the team too Dublin.
Expect plenty of Guinness, Irish stew and questionable footwear chat.
In Agony Ab, we hear from one of our listeners whose boyfriend’s hairline is in desperate need of a trip to Turkey, and learn why Bondi Rescue and Home and Away may not be the best research tools used if you are planning to emigrate to Australia.
So Happy (belated) St Patrick’s day, we hope you enjoy this week’s episode of the Therapy Crouch!
Sláinte!

programme.title: When good intentions backfire SPECTACULARLY
programme.desc:
After their recent break away from the kids to ‘disconnect and reconnect’ Abbey and Pete are back with tales of when even the best of intentions can backfire with hilarious results.
Thailand in the depths of monsoon season? Sounds like the perfect opportunity for Abbey to tear through three books and avoid talking to Pete at all costs…
In our weekly whine (or shine) club, we hear from a couple who have had the picture perfect proposal in nowhere else than the big apple itself as well as a woman who is convinced her husband has taken up whistling as a full time hobby just to drive her round the bend.
And we also hear some outrageous chat which will hopefully stand as a warning to all you guys on why you shouldn’t drunk and dial.
On this week’s Therapy Crouch we’ve got 99 problems but a b*tch aint one.
Enjoy!

programme.title: When Partying With Celebs Goes WRONG! Therapy Crouch #AMA
programme.desc:
On today’s episode of The Therapy Crouch #AMA, Abbey and Peter give a glimpse behind the curtain of what it’s really like partying with global superstars, and have some top tips on what to do when the party goes on a little too long….
By popular demand, Abbey is also revealing some of her tip top cleaning tips on how to get your house smelling just like the Crouch’s!
We hear from a listener who has concerns that her kids are knocking seven bells of sh*t out of each other on a regular basis and what best to do when your partner is going through the hormonal rollercoaster that is pregnancy
And stick around until the end when Abbey and Peter finally answer a MILLION DOLLAR QUESTION!
Enjoy this week’s, Therapy Crouch #AMA
If you want your question read out on the pod, hit the link below

programme.title: When You Are Too Old for Only Fans and Have to Create Your Own #OnlyGrans
programme.desc:
With one more week of the dreaded January blues left, we thought we would brighten up your day with a very special episode of The Therapy Crouch!
On today’s episode, Abbey and Peter are joined on the couch by Chattyman Alan Carr and his partner in crime Amanda Holden.
As you can probably guess, this episode was full of surprises, and explosive revelations. We hear Alans habits that drive Amanda up the wall, why you never want to take a trip to a marble quarry on a hangover and Alan’s disappointment at the fact he is less OnlyFans, more OnlyGrans….
They are also on hand to give advice to our listeners who are struggling with their ‘Mingebag’ husbands, groaning grannies and Judgey Dry Jan partners.
Enjoy this week’s, Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: “Thanks Mark, You’ve Ruined the Pod!” Abbey and Pete’s #AMA
programme.desc:
Welcome back to The Therapy Crouch: Ask Us Anything series, the only place where you can get Abbey and Pete’s raw and unfiltered answers to YOUR burning questions!
On today’s episode it is almost over before it even begins after a cheeky question from listener Mark threatens to derail the whole thing!!
Another listener also gets in touch who has a great addition to one of our new favourite games which leads to some outrageous film titles reimagined!
And stick around until the end, where Abbey gives her verdict on her top podcast recommendations that will change your life!

programme.title: “I Want to Enrich my Own Life, No Disrespect” Abbey’s New Venture Announced!
programme.desc:
On today’s episode of The Therapy Crouch, Abbey has a VERY special announcement regarding an exciting new career move.
After admitting that she needs a little more enrichment in her life than the what she is getting from the lighthearted drivel we chat on the crouch couch, Abbey has launched a new podcast venture - Exhibit A.
The first episode with Mo Gawdat and Alice Law is OUT NOW watch it here:

programme.title: Here comes the Bride(maid) - The Therapy Crouch
programme.desc:
It has been a big week for Abbey - she finally popped her bridesmaid’s cherry for none other than her BFF Holly.
But before they spill the beans on the wedding of the year, they reminisce on what has been a rather eventful week. From halloween face paint fails to a Geoff Clancy fashion faux pas there is plenty to be unravelled here.
Abbey and Pete also take a surprising detour from the pod topic and reminisce on some of their school school day pranks and performances which brings a lot of happy memories flooding back.
In Agony Ab, we hear from a woman who has suffered the ultimate betrayal from her former best friend and we also hear from someone who got A LOT more than they bargained for when they went for their monthly mop chop.
Enjoy this week’s, Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Half Term from Hell
programme.desc:
Happy Therapy Crouch release day! In this week’s episode Abbey and Peter talk about their rather eventful Easter break spent with their nearest and dearest….as well as their four kids.
With Pete pondering why the little ones get such a long time off for Easter when Jesus himself only took 3 days, Abbey lets loose on some of their less-than-wholesome strolls in the park and trips to the beach.
During Weekly Whine Club, Pete’s shortcomings on all things technology based are exposed and we hear from one of our listeners whose husband is left rather unimpressed by one of her morning rituals.
In Agony Ab, we hear how one of our dear listeners is planning on throwing the wedding of a lifetime with his boyhood passion taking front and centre stage, and what to do when your gut feeling is telling you there is something wrong in the relationship.
We hope you enjoy this weeks episode of The Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Guilty Pleasures
programme.desc:
After the ribena-gate of last week, tune in to see if Ralphie made it to a second week in the Crouch family household.
This week, the Abbey and Peter are chatting about all things guilty pleasures. After hearing about a recent TikTok trend, Abbey is desperate to stay down with the kids and expose all the things that men secretly love but hate to admit, what could go wrong…..?
From their mutual taste in TV and the fact they are both more than partial to a bit of Simply Red, it is safe to say they both have more than their fair share of guilty pleasures they can expose the other one for.
As always, you guys have been in touch in search of some advice from the nation’s number 1 Agony AB! Abbey takes the role of hype queen for a woman who has been stuck with a guy suffering from Botox Bias and is on hand to help a young gent who is suffering from a 3 ft 5 neighbour from hell.

programme.title: Glastonbury....Part 1
programme.desc:
Welcome back to The Therapy Crouch! This week, your favourite couple take a sit down to spill the beans on all their Glastonbury planning and preparations. We have got some interesting ideas on how we can help Pete go incognito in the crowd (wig suggestions welcome) and Abbey gives a breakdown on how she is going to give Sir Elton John himself a run for his money when it comes to outfit changes.
In Weekly Whine Club you guys have been in touch to berate Peter about his skinny wrists and we also hear how you have incorporated this into your own daily lives with varying degrees of success.
Agony Ab is also back…but this time with a twist! Rather than our usual pearls of wisdom and relationship saving advice, we hear some of your best (and worst) festival stories….from burst sh*t pipes and POONCHO nightmares - nothing is off the cards.
Enjoy this week’s, Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Friends with (no) Benefits
programme.desc:
ADVISORY CONTENT WARNING: Unfortunately, our mics dropped out for the last 10 mins of this week’s episode so we apologise for the audio quality at the end of the ep......
Welcome to this week’s episode of the Therapy Crouch!
This week, Abbey has had some life affirming news and it has encouraged the gang to discuss all things friendship group related.
Ever found yourself stuck with a talkative boring Norman at a wedding? Or had the dreaded experience of when two of your close friends split up and you don’t know which one to keep? You’re not alone.
Abbey and Peter are here to give you some witty personal anecdotes, and advice on what to do if you ever find yourself in one of these sticky situations.
We also find out why Pete could be inline for the next Gucci modelling job and Abbey has some choice words for one of our listeners who has an issue with her attempt at an Italian accent.
It’s a cracker!

programme.title: Freaky Friday
programme.desc:
In this week’s Therapy Crouch, Abbey and Peter embrace their inner Jamie Lee Curtis and Lindsey Lohan and ponder one of life’s great questions…..what would you do if you found yourself in a Freaky Friday situation?
They look at which one of their heroes they would most like to switch with, what would be the first thing they’d do if they found themselves in the other’s body (parental guidance advised) and Peter answers a very tricky question regarding his mum and Abbey’s own Freaky Friday experience.
In Agony Ab, we hear from the only woman on the planet who is offended her husband believes she resembles Margot Robbie and Pete tries to help a sister out who’s in desperate need of some funny one liners for her Tinder profile.
Enjoy this week’s Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Father's Day
programme.desc:
On this week’s Therapy Crouch, Abbey and Peter sit down and have a chat about their recent Father’s Day festivities, with Pete having put in a slightly underwhelming performance on Mother’s Day, is it likely Abbey pulled out all the stops for him this year? There is only one way to find out!
To kick things off, Abbey and Peter share with us some of their favourite Dad jokes (that’s rubbish jokes, not jokes about Dads, Peter)
In Agony Ab, the gang are on hand to give some advice for when your best mate gets a new girlfriend and she is constantly up staging you in the best friend department. Abbey is also on hand for some reassuring advice for one of our listeners who has recently seen one of her flings develop into something more serious with potentially catastrophic consequences.
So sit back, relax and enjoy this weeks Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Doggystyle
programme.desc:
In this week’s pod, Peter finally admits his love love for the young pup!! As expected, Abbey is less than magnanimous in success but there is no love lost between the pair.
In the weekly whine club, one of our golf widow listeners has been in touch, much to the detriment of the big man, and we also hear from a young scaffolder who’s struggling to enjoy a day evening off even on a cold wet Tuesday underneath the spotlights.
In one of our most F’d up Agony Abs of the series, a long time suffering housewife details her husbands perverted tea making skills and Abbey is on hand to give some advice to an unlucky in love recently singleton.
Enjoy this week’s Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: David Walliams Talks Swimming the Channel, Austin Powers, Matt Lucas and Astrochimp!
programme.desc:
On today’s episode of The Therapy Crouch, Abbey and Pete are joined by actor, comedian and children’s author David Walliams who heard us shouting him out on a previous pod and decided to pop onto the couch for a chat!
David talks about his massive success in the world of children’s literature, his literary inspirations and why writing two books is never the same process.
He also weighs in on Abbey’s debut novel ‘Remember my name’ and gives a very special reading of the prologue which now has Abbey pondering if we should get this re released as an audio book!
In the Agony Ab section, we have a Shallow Hal-esqu scenario featuring a boyfriend who is getting worried about his girlfriend waistline woes and we have some very direct advice for a listener whose girls trip is in jeopardy due to the addition of some unwanted plus ones!

programme.title: “Thanks Mark, You’ve Ruined the Pod!” Abbey and Pete’s #AMA
programme.desc:
Welcome back to The Therapy Crouch: Ask Us Anything series, the only place where you can get Abbey and Pete’s raw and unfiltered answers to YOUR burning questions!
On today’s episode it is almost over before it even begins after a cheeky question from listener Mark threatens to derail the whole thing!!
Another listener also gets in touch who has a great addition to one of our new favourite games which leads to some outrageous film titles reimagined!
And stick around until the end, where Abbey gives her verdict on her top podcast recommendations that will change your life!

programme.title: Therapy Crouchfest
programme.desc:
On today’s episode of The Therapy Crouch, Abbey and Peter take a while to take stock after the roaring success that was Crouchfest.
Never ones to take themselves too seriously, the gang talk all things fancy dress, gameshow parodies, music and everything in between.
Abbey also gives insight into their ‘Sunday Monday’ experience - a rare treat when the house is normally jam packed with four kids.
In the Agony Abs, we hear from a listener who is feeling pressured into going on a rather expensive family group holiday as well as a young couple who are struggling to decide if they want to have another baby, secure their nest or blow it all on the big day!
Enjoy this week’s, Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: “I Want to Enrich my Own Life, No Disrespect” Abbey’s New Venture Announced!
programme.desc:
On today’s episode of The Therapy Crouch, Abbey has a VERY special announcement regarding an exciting new career move.
After admitting that she needs a little more enrichment in her life than the what she is getting from the lighthearted drivel we chat on the crouch couch, Abbey has launched a new podcast venture - Exhibit A.
The first episode with Mo Gawdat and Alice Law is OUT NOW watch it here:

programme.title: Youth is Wasted on the Young
programme.desc:
It’s your favourite part of the week again! Therapy Crouch is back with a bang and this week Abbey and Peter lift the lid on some of their misspent youth activities.
Expect tales of underage discos enough to whip you up into a FRENZY and lessons how not to pull a sicky when you are battling your first hangover.
Agony Ab is on hand to provide some much needed guidance and comic relief for what to do when your husband has some interesting locations he likes to spend a penny or, even worse, thinks they’re the next Whitney Houston.
Enjoy this week’s Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: When Partying With Celebs Goes WRONG! Therapy Crouch #AMA
programme.desc:
On today’s episode of The Therapy Crouch #AMA, Abbey and Peter give a glimpse behind the curtain of what it’s really like partying with global superstars, and have some top tips on what to do when the party goes on a little too long….
By popular demand, Abbey is also revealing some of her tip top cleaning tips on how to get your house smelling just like the Crouch’s!
We hear from a listener who has concerns that her kids are knocking seven bells of sh*t out of each other on a regular basis and what best to do when your partner is going through the hormonal rollercoaster that is pregnancy
And stick around until the end when Abbey and Peter finally answer a MILLION DOLLAR QUESTION!
Enjoy this week’s, Therapy Crouch #AMA
If you want your question read out on the pod, hit the link below

programme.title: When good intentions backfire SPECTACULARLY
programme.desc:
After their recent break away from the kids to ‘disconnect and reconnect’ Abbey and Pete are back with tales of when even the best of intentions can backfire with hilarious results.
Thailand in the depths of monsoon season? Sounds like the perfect opportunity for Abbey to tear through three books and avoid talking to Pete at all costs…
In our weekly whine (or shine) club, we hear from a couple who have had the picture perfect proposal in nowhere else than the big apple itself as well as a woman who is convinced her husband has taken up whistling as a full time hobby just to drive her round the bend.
And we also hear some outrageous chat which will hopefully stand as a warning to all you guys on why you shouldn’t drunk and dial.
On this week’s Therapy Crouch we’ve got 99 problems but a b*tch aint one.
Enjoy!

programme.title: Wedding Planning
programme.desc:
On today’s episode, we get an exclusive invite to the planning behind the wedding of the decade. From pre-match nerves to Elton John’s florist - learn more about the planning of the most important day of Abbey’s life (and probably Pete’s as well).
We also hear how Abbey feels how she has made a load of new friends since starting the podcast and why Peter is still struggling to show his face in the bakery after THAT famous incident.
And your favourite Agony Ab is back, helping to navigate the tricky waters of what to do when your girlfriend plans, and pays for, your wedding without your knowledge but on your credit card and how to get out of your sister’s OTT wedding prep.

programme.title: To Dream The Impossible Dream
programme.desc:
In this week’s Therapy Crouch, Abbey and Peter take a while to sit down and discuss some of their dreams and aspirations both as a pair of youngsters and now as one of the UK’s most prominent power couples.
We hear about Abbey’s early days in Genie Queen - Liverpool’s answer to the Sugababes - and a young football protege with dreams of scoring 100 premier league goals and becoming the nation’s Podfather in the process.
We also hear from our lovely listeners with their Weekly Whines and Agony Abs. We hear about one of listeners whose girlfriend has being using their toothbrush for bit of DIY and a fellow NETTO alumni has been in touch with a playground anthem that has been sung for generations across Merseyside.
Enjoy this week’s Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: They Call Me The Godfather
programme.desc:
Welcome back to The Therapy Crouch! On this week’s episode Abbey and Peter are on hand to share some pearls of wisdom on what makes a good godparent, and some top tips on how to expertly navigate a thanks but no thanks conversation with your nearest and dearest.
In Agony Ab, Abbey and Peter are on hand to help when your better half suddenly decides to take up the gym 10 years too late and we hear from Louis, one of our international listeners who needs some advice on how to make the first move!
We hope you enjoy this very special episode from the OG Podfather and your fairy Podmother!

programme.title: The Therapy Crouch made some new friends....and they do not disappoint
programme.desc:
The moment you’ve all been waiting for has finally arrived! The Therapy Crouch has waited almost a year for this very special moment and boy was it worth it…
This week, we are joined by fellow podcasters and new best friends, James Buckley and Clair Buckley!
It’s fair to say that this is a bit of a milestone for the podcast and nothing was off the table in this no holds barred chat, from how to pity a girl into bed to James’ truly teeth woes.
They are also a welcome addition to Agony Ab couch, where we hear from a woman who is being constantly undermined in the workplace which Clair has some FANTASTIC advice for.
Do not miss this one!

programme.title: The Therapy Crouch - You May As Well Move In Then
programme.desc:
On this week’s episode, Pete provides a lesson on exactly how not to ask the love of your life to move in with you.
Learn more about Peter’s revolutionary invention, ‘The Squat Box’ and why you should never leave your home movie collection lying around when your future wife and mother-in-law are coming round for tea.
From Pet Detectives to interior design clashes, no stone is left unturned on The Therapy Crouch.

programme.title: The Therapy Crouch - Will You Marry Me?
programme.desc:
This week, Abbey and Peter provide a schooling session on how to nail the perfect proposal (spoiler: It does not involve chemtrails)
Weekly Whine club is also back, with Abbey and Peter going a bit more upmarket to celebrate the roaring success that is The Therapy Crouch! We learn why you can't trust Abbey to keep a clean car and how Peter is considering taking up a career modelling fake tan.
And, as always, Agony Ab is on hand to give you the listener some advice for your tricky relationship problems.
Enjoy!

programme.title: The Therapy Crouch - I WANT BONO
programme.desc:
On today’s episode, Abbey and Peter shed light on all the things they have in common, from nights in watching boxsets to nights out on the tiles.... and even their taste in men.
The pair also bond over their shared appreciation for all things Bono and Barry from Eastenders.
In the Weekly Whine Club, we learn why having a Victoria Secret’s model as a wife may not be as glamorous as it sounds and, rather ironically, that poor punctuality is one of Abbey’s pet hates.
In Agony Ab, we go international and provide assistance to some of our Spanish listeners and Pete gives tips on how to keep to spice alive (spoiler: it does not involve dressing up as a viking)

programme.title: The Sidwell’s: Steve and Krysy Sidwell on Marriage, Teenage Tear-Ups and Book Club Blunders
programme.desc:
On today’s episode of The Therapy Crouch, Abbey and Peter are joined by some of their oldest and closest friends - Steve and Krysy Sidwell.
In a friendship spanning two decades which has seen the couples share career paths, holidays and some outrageous nights out, this podcast is full of laughs and a behind the curtain, sneak peak into what it’s like being best friends with your favourite pair of podcasters!
Steve and Krysy discuss what they think has made their 20 year marriage stay rock solid after all these years and how they are navigating having three teenage boys who have developed of penchant for knocking seven bells of sh*t out of each other on a regular basis.
In the Agony Ab section, we hear from a listener who has some concerns about his wife’s recent fashion choices as well what to do in the awkward situation when someone likes you more than you like them - so stay tuned for that!!
Enjoy this week’s Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: David Walliams Talks Swimming the Channel, Austin Powers, Matt Lucas and Astrochimp!
programme.desc:

David talks about his massive success in the world of children’s literature, his literary inspirations and why writing two books is never the same process.

In the Agony Ab section, we have a Shallow Hal-esqu scenario featuring a boyfriend who is getting worried about his girlfriend waistline woes and we have some very direct advice for a listener whose girls trip is in jeopardy due to the addition of some unwanted plus ones!

programme.title: Doggystyle
programme.desc:
