In the weekly whine club, one of our golf widow listeners has been in touch, much to the detriment of the big man, and we also hear from a young scaffolder who’s struggling to enjoy a day evening off even on a cold wet Tuesday underneath the spotlights.
In one of our most F’d up Agony Abs of the series, a long time suffering housewife details her husbands perverted tea making skills and Abbey is on hand to give some advice to an unlucky in love recently singleton.
Enjoy this week’s Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Father's Day
programme.desc:
On this week’s Therapy Crouch, Abbey and Peter sit down and have a chat about their recent Father’s Day festivities, with Pete having put in a slightly underwhelming performance on Mother’s Day, is it likely Abbey pulled out all the stops for him this year? There is only one way to find out!
To kick things off, Abbey and Peter share with us some of their favourite Dad jokes (that’s rubbish jokes, not jokes about Dads, Peter)
In Agony Ab, the gang are on hand to give some advice for when your best mate gets a new girlfriend and she is constantly up staging you in the best friend department. Abbey is also on hand for some reassuring advice for one of our listeners who has recently seen one of her flings develop into something more serious with potentially catastrophic consequences.
So sit back, relax and enjoy this weeks Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Freaky Friday
programme.desc:
In this week’s Therapy Crouch, Abbey and Peter embrace their inner Jamie Lee Curtis and Lindsey Lohan and ponder one of life’s great questions…..what would you do if you found yourself in a Freaky Friday situation?
They look at which one of their heroes they would most like to switch with, what would be the first thing they’d do if they found themselves in the other’s body (parental guidance advised) and Peter answers a very tricky question regarding his mum and Abbey’s own Freaky Friday experience.
In Agony Ab, we hear from the only woman on the planet who is offended her husband believes she resembles Margot Robbie and Pete tries to help a sister out who’s in desperate need of some funny one liners for her Tinder profile.
Enjoy this week’s Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Friends with (no) Benefits
programme.desc:
ADVISORY CONTENT WARNING: Unfortunately, our mics dropped out for the last 10 mins of this week’s episode so we apologise for the audio quality at the end of the ep......
Welcome to this week’s episode of the Therapy Crouch!
This week, Abbey has had some life affirming news and it has encouraged the gang to discuss all things friendship group related.
Ever found yourself stuck with a talkative boring Norman at a wedding? Or had the dreaded experience of when two of your close friends split up and you don’t know which one to keep? You’re not alone.
Abbey and Peter are here to give you some witty personal anecdotes, and advice on what to do if you ever find yourself in one of these sticky situations.
We also find out why Pete could be inline for the next Gucci modelling job and Abbey has some choice words for one of our listeners who has an issue with her attempt at an Italian accent.
It’s a cracker!

programme.title: Glastonbury....Part 1
programme.desc:
Welcome back to The Therapy Crouch! This week, your favourite couple take a sit down to spill the beans on all their Glastonbury planning and preparations. We have got some interesting ideas on how we can help Pete go incognito in the crowd (wig suggestions welcome) and Abbey gives a breakdown on how she is going to give Sir Elton John himself a run for his money when it comes to outfit changes.
In Weekly Whine Club you guys have been in touch to berate Peter about his skinny wrists and we also hear how you have incorporated this into your own daily lives with varying degrees of success.
Agony Ab is also back…but this time with a twist! Rather than our usual pearls of wisdom and relationship saving advice, we hear some of your best (and worst) festival stories….from burst sh*t pipes and POONCHO nightmares - nothing is off the cards.
Enjoy this week’s, Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Glasto....Part 2
programme.desc:
It’s the one you have all been waiting for! After a rather hectic Glastonbury experience, a slightly worse for wear Therapy Crouch is here to dish the dirt on what went down at Worthy Farm.
The gang battle through some rather self-inflicted hangovers and lift the lid on their Glastonbury experience - expect a lot of Elton John chat, Abbey’s step count and the avoidance of the dreaded long drop….
Enjoy this week’s Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Half Term from Hell
programme.desc:
Happy Therapy Crouch release day! In this week’s episode Abbey and Peter talk about their rather eventful Easter break spent with their nearest and dearest….as well as their four kids.
With Pete pondering why the little ones get such a long time off for Easter when Jesus himself only took 3 days, Abbey lets loose on some of their less-than-wholesome strolls in the park and trips to the beach.
During Weekly Whine Club, Pete’s shortcomings on all things technology based are exposed and we hear from one of our listeners whose husband is left rather unimpressed by one of her morning rituals.
In Agony Ab, we hear how one of our dear listeners is planning on throwing the wedding of a lifetime with his boyhood passion taking front and centre stage, and what to do when your gut feeling is telling you there is something wrong in the relationship.
We hope you enjoy this weeks episode of The Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Here comes the Bride(maid) - The Therapy Crouch
programme.desc:
It has been a big week for Abbey - she finally popped her bridesmaid’s cherry for none other than her BFF Holly.
But before they spill the beans on the wedding of the year, they reminisce on what has been a rather eventful week. From halloween face paint fails to a Geoff Clancy fashion faux pas there is plenty to be unravelled here.
Abbey and Pete also take a surprising detour from the pod topic and reminisce on some of their school school day pranks and performances which brings a lot of happy memories flooding back.
In Agony Ab, we hear from a woman who has suffered the ultimate betrayal from her former best friend and we also hear from someone who got A LOT more than they bargained for when they went for their monthly mop chop.
Enjoy this week’s, Therapy Crouch!

programme.desc:

Although it isn’t all festive cheer as the pair have been up to their usual antics and have come locked and loaded with multiple weekly whines - a lot of of them regarding the other’s fashion faux pas.

programme.desc:

Expect plenty of Guinness, Irish stew and questionable footwear chat.
In Agony Ab, we hear from one of our listeners whose boyfriend’s hairline is in desperate need of a trip to Turkey, and learn why Bondi Rescue and Home and Away may not be the best research tools used if you are planning to emigrate to Australia.
So Happy (belated) St Patrick’s day, we hope you enjoy this week’s episode of the Therapy Crouch!
Sláinte!

programme.title: When You Are Too Old for Only Fans and Have to Create Your Own #OnlyGrans
programme.desc:
With one more week of the dreaded January blues left, we thought we would brighten up your day with a very special episode of The Therapy Crouch!
On today’s episode, Abbey and Peter are joined on the couch by Chattyman Alan Carr and his partner in crime Amanda Holden.
As you can probably guess, this episode was full of surprises, and explosive revelations. We hear Alans habits that drive Amanda up the wall, why you never want to take a trip to a marble quarry on a hangover and Alan’s disappointment at the fact he is less OnlyFans, more OnlyGrans….

programme.desc:
In this week’s Therapy Crouch, Abbey and Peter embrace their inner Jamie Lee Curtis and Lindsey Lohan and ponder one of life’s great questions…..what would you do if you found yourself in a Freaky Friday situation?
They look at which one of their heroes they would most like to switch with, what would be the first thing they’d do if they found themselves in the other’s body (parental guidance advised) and Peter answers a very tricky question regarding his mum and Abbey’s own Freaky Friday experience.
In Agony Ab, we hear from the only woman on the planet who is offended her husband believes she resembles Margot Robbie and Pete tries to help a sister out who’s in desperate need of some funny one liners for her Tinder profile.
Enjoy this week’s Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Glastonbury....Part 1
programme.desc:
Welcome back to The Therapy Crouch! This week, your favourite couple take a sit down to spill the beans on all their Glastonbury planning and preparations. We have got some interesting ideas on how we can help Pete go incognito in the crowd (wig suggestions welcome) and Abbey gives a breakdown on how she is going to give Sir Elton John himself a run for his money when it comes to outfit changes.
In Weekly Whine Club you guys have been in touch to berate Peter about his skinny wrists and we also hear how you have incorporated this into your own daily lives with varying degrees of success.
Agony Ab is also back…but this time with a twist! Rather than our usual pearls of wisdom and relationship saving advice, we hear some of your best (and worst) festival stories….from burst sh*t pipes and POONCHO nightmares - nothing is off the cards.
Enjoy this week’s, Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Glasto....Part 2
programme.desc:
It’s the one you have all been waiting for! After a rather hectic Glastonbury experience, a slightly worse for wear Therapy Crouch is here to dish the dirt on what went down at Worthy Farm.
The gang battle through some rather self-inflicted hangovers and lift the lid on their Glastonbury experience - expect a lot of Elton John chat, Abbey’s step count and the avoidance of the dreaded long drop….
Enjoy this week’s Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Guilty Pleasures
programme.desc:
After the ribena-gate of last week, tune in to see if Ralphie made it to a second week in the Crouch family household.
This week, the Abbey and Peter are chatting about all things guilty pleasures. After hearing about a recent TikTok trend, Abbey is desperate to stay down with the kids and expose all the things that men secretly love but hate to admit, what could go wrong…..?
From their mutual taste in TV and the fact they are both more than partial to a bit of Simply Red, it is safe to say they both have more than their fair share of guilty pleasures they can expose the other one for.
As always, you guys have been in touch in search of some advice from the nation’s number 1 Agony AB! Abbey takes the role of hype queen for a woman who has been stuck with a guy suffering from Botox Bias and is on hand to help a young gent who is suffering from a 3 ft 5 neighbour from hell.

programme.title: Happy Halloween
programme.desc:
Keeping in the spirit of the spookiest day of the year, Abbey and Peter have donned their best outfits and are talking all things fright night.
Before delving into everything that gives them the heebie jeebies though, we have a recap on what could have potentially been the best week of Abbey’s life……so far.
There is some big name dropping in this week’s podcast, with Madonna, Frankie Dettori, Mick Hucknall of Simply Rouge and Mr Micky Mouse himself all getting honourable mentions.
It’s a laugh a minute on today’s Therapy Crouch, with Dad jokes galore, Pete’s biggest fears exposed and Abbey’s sharp scouse wit is in full swing to keep everyone in check.
In our Agony Ab section, Abbey and Pete are on hand to give advice on toenail health and maintenance as well as what to do when your hubby starts to neglect his hair and waistlines!
Happy Halloween from all the team at The Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Here comes the Bride(maid) - The Therapy Crouch
programme.desc:
It has been a big week for Abbey - she finally popped her bridesmaid’s cherry for none other than her BFF Holly.
But before they spill the beans on the wedding of the year, they reminisce on what has been a rather eventful week. From halloween face paint fails to a Geoff Clancy fashion faux pas there is plenty to be unravelled here.
Abbey and Pete also take a surprising detour from the pod topic and reminisce on some of their school school day pranks and performances which brings a lot of happy memories flooding back.
In Agony Ab, we hear from a woman who has suffered the ultimate betrayal from her former best friend and we also hear from someone who got A LOT more than they bargained for when they went for their monthly mop chop.
Enjoy this week’s, Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: “I Want to Enrich my Own Life, No Disrespect” Abbey’s New Venture Announced!
programme.desc:
On today’s episode of The Therapy Crouch, Abbey has a VERY special announcement regarding an exciting new career move.
After admitting that she needs a little more enrichment in her life than the what she is getting from the lighthearted drivel we chat on the crouch couch, Abbey has launched a new podcast venture - Exhibit A.
The first episode with Mo Gawdat and Alice Law is OUT NOW watch it here:

programme.title: Glastonbury....Part 1
programme.desc:
Welcome back to The Therapy Crouch! This week, your favourite couple take a sit down to spill the beans on all their Glastonbury planning and preparations. We have got some interesting ideas on how we can help Pete go incognito in the crowd (wig suggestions welcome) and Abbey gives a breakdown on how she is going to give Sir Elton John himself a run for his money when it comes to outfit changes.
In Weekly Whine Club you guys have been in touch to berate Peter about his skinny wrists and we also hear how you have incorporated this into your own daily lives with varying degrees of success.
Agony Ab is also back…but this time with a twist! Rather than our usual pearls of wisdom and relationship saving advice, we hear some of your best (and worst) festival stories….from burst sh*t pipes and POONCHO nightmares - nothing is off the cards.
Enjoy this week’s, Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Glasto....Part 2
programme.desc:
It’s the one you have all been waiting for! After a rather hectic Glastonbury experience, a slightly worse for wear Therapy Crouch is here to dish the dirt on what went down at Worthy Farm.
The gang battle through some rather self-inflicted hangovers and lift the lid on their Glastonbury experience - expect a lot of Elton John chat, Abbey’s step count and the avoidance of the dreaded long drop….
Enjoy this week’s Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Friends with (no) Benefits
programme.desc:
ADVISORY CONTENT WARNING: Unfortunately, our mics dropped out for the last 10 mins of this week’s episode so we apologise for the audio quality at the end of the ep......
Welcome to this week’s episode of the Therapy Crouch!
This week, Abbey has had some life affirming news and it has encouraged the gang to discuss all things friendship group related.
Ever found yourself stuck with a talkative boring Norman at a wedding? Or had the dreaded experience of when two of your close friends split up and you don’t know which one to keep? You’re not alone.
Abbey and Peter are here to give you some witty personal anecdotes, and advice on what to do if you ever find yourself in one of these sticky situations.
We also find out why Pete could be inline for the next Gucci modelling job and Abbey has some choice words for one of our listeners who has an issue with her attempt at an Italian accent.
It’s a cracker!

programme.title: Father's Day
programme.desc:
On this week’s Therapy Crouch, Abbey and Peter sit down and have a chat about their recent Father’s Day festivities, with Pete having put in a slightly underwhelming performance on Mother’s Day, is it likely Abbey pulled out all the stops for him this year? There is only one way to find out!
To kick things off, Abbey and Peter share with us some of their favourite Dad jokes (that’s rubbish jokes, not jokes about Dads, Peter)
In Agony Ab, the gang are on hand to give some advice for when your best mate gets a new girlfriend and she is constantly up staging you in the best friend department. Abbey is also on hand for some reassuring advice for one of our listeners who has recently seen one of her flings develop into something more serious with potentially catastrophic consequences.
So sit back, relax and enjoy this weeks Therapy Crouch!

programme.title: Extraterrestrials, Memory Loss and Bedroom Antics: Therapy Crouch #AMA
programme.desc:
On today’s episode of The Therapy Crouch, Ask Us Anything, Abbey and Peter are back answering some of the hot topics that you guys want to know the answer too!
As is quickly becoming a bit of a tradition of this new weekly edition, we hear some weird and wonderful ‘would you rather’ questions the majority of which leave Abbey and Pete stumped on how you guys even come up with them!
We also tackle some more hard hitting questions, such as sleep talking, how to keep yourself occupied in the bedroom on a cold rainy day, and Abbey finally puts to bed the age old question of is their Alien life out there?
Enjoy this week’s Therapy Crouch, Ask Us Anything!

programme.desc: Matt and Froy walk into a moral gray area when they repossess a vehicle by stealing it.

programme.title: Four Wheel Surprise
programme.desc: Lou and Matt confront a big-time player and his bodyguards at a Brazilian restaurant.

programme.title: Breakdowns and Beatdowns
programme.desc: Matt and Froy face gang bangers, a teacher's brawl, and a tow truck breakdown.

programme.title: You Again?
programme.desc: Sonia and Lou face an angry doctor, a furious athlete, and a minister's van repo with unexpected trouble.

programme.title: Scavenger Hunt
programme.desc: Lou and Froy prank bikers, Sonia and Matt repo a tanning bed, and a sting on a shady tow driver takes a twist.

programme.title: The Dune Buggy
programme.desc: Lou and Froy face snobby diners, Lyndah and Froy repo a van from thieves, and Matt nabs a rare dune buggy.

programme.title: High and Low Spirits
programme.desc: Lou and Sonia face a wild love triangle, Sonia repos a designer's van.

programme.title: Repo Outside the Box
programme.desc: Matt and Sonia face violent cooks, Froy repos a model's van, and a street gang outmatches the team.

programme.title: Horse Semen & the Rolls Royce Golf Cart
programme.desc: Froy, Sonia repossess truck; handle flower dispute; reclaim Rolls Royce golf cart.

programme.title: Involuntary Voluntary
programme.desc: Lou and Matt expose a cheating husband's scheme, repossess a drunk debtor's car, and confront an armed driver.

programme.title: Froy's Bait and Lou's Cake
programme.desc: Lou & Froy bait a debtor, face pepper spray, crash a wedding for a DJ's car, & confront bikers.

programme.title: Blow-Up and Cool Down
programme.desc: Lou & Lyndah repo a mower, Sonia & Matt face heat & elderly debtors, & a shocking secret is found in a car.

programme.title: The Car That Rolled Away
programme.desc: Lou & Lyndah face drunk outdoorsmen, Froy & Lyndah repo a bad guitarist, and Froy & Sonia confront an ex-NFL.

programme.title: Naked and Dangerous
programme.desc: Sonia & Lyndah repo a hot tub, Lou & Matt face angry paraders, and Lou & Lyndah take a car from old men.

programme.title: Hooligans and Explosions
programme.desc: A pan-wielding housewife fights Lou & Sonia, while Matt & Froy's repo of a contraband-filled truck goes wrong.

programme.title: Reading, Painting, and Physical Education
programme.desc: Froy & Sonia repo a Corvette, Matt & Froy face feuding brothers, & Lou & Matt take a bus from a baseball team

programme.title: Heavy Hitters
programme.desc: Lou, Matt & Froy retry a bus repo & take a crane, while Sonia & Froy are chased by bikers to the office.

programme.title: Ruffians and Wrestlers
programme.desc: Sonia & Matt face skaters, Froy & Sonia repo from a volatile couple, & Lou & Froy take on backyard wrestlers.

programme.title: Everybody Just a Child?
programme.desc: First, Matt and Froy get the worst service ever from a street-side coffee vendor who won't give up his truck.

programme.title: Off the Hitch
programme.desc: In this very special episode of Operation Repo, it's the 36 hours that change everything.

programme.title: Matt & Froy Auto Recovery
programme.desc: First, Lou and Sonia make their way to a home in the hills to clean a germaphobe of his car.

programme.title: Win Some, Quarrelsome, Lose Some
programme.desc: First, Lou and Sonia have an order for a Jaguar behind a retail outlet center.

programme.title: We Weren't Informed
programme.desc: Matt and Froy end up on one of the weirdest repo's they've ever encountered.

programme.title: Tijuana Surprise
programme.desc: Matt and Froy's business hits another snag when a gang of bat-wielding partiers keeps them away.

programme.title: Lyndah's Revenge
programme.desc: Matt and Froy continue their risky run in Tijuana, confronting a strip club owner who keeps a tight grip.

programme.title: El Prisionero
programme.desc: Lou and his cousin Rob face trouble on the job when a debtor makes shocking claims to police.

programme.title: The Final Showdown
programme.desc: After Froy bails him out from a Tijuana prison, Matt is on the warpath, and somebody needs to pay.

programme.title: What the Truck?!?
programme.desc: Lou, Matt, Sonia, Froy, and Lyndah are back together and better than ever.

programme.title: Contact
programme.desc: After reclaiming their stolen truck, the repo team struggles to keep their fragile alliance intact.

programme.title: The Crush
programme.desc: Lou faces his toughest loss, repossessing a truck, a tricked-out bus, and tracking a debtor to a junkyard.

programme.title: HEAL
programme.desc: Director Kelly Noonan Gores’ HEAL takes us on a scientific and spiritual journey where we discover that our thoughts, beliefs, and emotions have a huge impact on our health and ability to heal. We have more control over our health and life than we have been taught to believe.

programme.title: Play To Win - Play To Win- Episode 4
programme.desc: Getting attention for yourself or for a product is one of the most difficult things you will ever be faced with. We devised a unique marketing challenge unlike any of the previous episodes.

programme.title: HEAL
programme.desc: Director Kelly Noonan Gores’ HEAL takes us on a scientific and spiritual journey where we discover that our thoughts, beliefs, and emotions have a huge impact on our health and ability to heal. We have more control over our health and life than we have been taught to believe.

programme.title: Sex Talk - Sex Talk - Keeping the Sexual Fires Burning
programme.desc: Want to keep the fires burning even when you’re too tired? Got questions about how long ED medications last? We break it down on Sex Talk with Dr. Lia.

programme.title: Sex Talk - Sex Talk - Hot Sex Even Without Orgasm
programme.desc: Keeping it sexy between the sheets is possible even without the big “O” and a man wonders if he can get intimate post-heart attack. All this and more on Sex Talk with Dr. Lia.

programme.title: The Aware Show - The Aware Show: Robert Thurman - Wisdom is Bliss
programme.desc: Host Lisa Garr interviews Professor Robert Thurman, the preeminent scholar and interpreter of Tibetan Buddhist philosophy for the modern world, who believes that Wisdom is Bliss and in fact, that’s the name of his latest book. Thurman is a very close friend of HH Dalai Lama and today he discusses deep ideas about mindfulness and presence and leads us on a joyful exploration into the nature of reality through Buddha’s threefold curriculum.

programme.title: The Aware Show - The Aware Show: Dr Harley Rotbart - No Regrets Parenting
programme.desc: Host Lisa Garr interviews Dr. Harley Rotbart, a nationally renowned infectious diseases specialist, pediatrician, parenting expert, speaker, and educator who discusses his latest book No Regrets Parenting: Turning Long Days and Short Years into Cherished Moments with Your Kids. He shares special keys for parents to guide children to live a life of wonder and contentment by conscious parenting.

programme.title: The Aware Show - The Aware Show: Dr. Michael Tobin - Secrets to a Loving Relationship
programme.desc: Host Lisa Garr interviews marital and family psychologist Dr. Michael Tobin who believes the secret to a happy, long-lasting relationship is mutual respect for one another. He’s been with his life partner, soulmate, and wife, Deborah, for 47 years, and in practice as a psychologist for more than 45 years. His most recent book, Riding the Edge, A Love Song to Deborah, chronicles his six-month transformative journey.

programme.title: The Aware Show - The Aware Show: Dr. James Greenblatt --- Nutritional Solutions in Psychiatry
programme.desc: Host Lisa Garr interviews Dr. James Greenblatt, a pioneer in the field of integrative medicine who has been treating patients with eating disorders for many decades. His nutritional approach to psychiatry is revolutionary and has helped patients worldwide. He is the author of seven books, including Answers to Anorexia and Finally Focused: The Breakthrough Natural Treatment Plan for ADHD.

programme.title: Dr. Q&A - Dr. Q&A - The Truth About Prostate Cancer
programme.desc: Prostate cancer is the most common cancer in men. But there is a great deal of misinformation about treatments, causes and even screening. Find out the truth from the expert in this program.

programme.title: Dr. Q&A - Dr. Q&A- Arthritis and Pain Relief
programme.desc: Arthritis is the most common cause of hip problems but overuse and injury can also lead to pain, stiffness, and weakness. We ask an expert about what you can do to feel relief.

programme.title: Dr. Q&A - Dr. Q&A- Sleep
programme.desc: We spend a third of our lives fast asleep, but it’s not always so easy to get there. We ask an expert about how you can get a good night’s rest.

programme.title: Dr. Q&A - Dr. Q&A - Sun & Your Skin
programme.desc: Sun exposure increases your risk of skin damage. We break down these common skin issues and what you can do about them.

programme.title: Dr. Q&A - Dr. Q&A - Youth Mental Health
programme.desc: It’s often difficult to talk about mental illness with a child or teenager. We talk to an expert to help guide you through the conversation.

programme.title: Dr. Q&A - Dr. Q&A- Cancer Diagnosis and Treatment
programme.desc: Breast cancer researcher and physician talks about the latest therapies used to treat breast cancer and the advances that are bringing hope and healing to millions of people because of clinical trials.

programme.title: Good Morning Joy - Good Morning Joy - Unlocking Happiness
programme.desc: Celebrity hypnotist Tim Shurr helps you unlock happiness and shed anxiety. Shari tests her hypnotizability, and the story of a woman who took back her mental health by empowering women worldwide. Plus, live from Heartfulness headquarters in India.

programme.title: Good Morning Joy - Good Morning Joy - Triumph Over Trauma
programme.desc: 1963 Playboy Icon and Cosby Survivor, Victoria Valentino, shares her journey of overcoming unimaginable circumstances leading her to activism and purpose. Plus, one man's heroic efforts to save a tiny, unconscious life, and fun street interviews from NYC.

programme.title: Good Morning Joy - Good Morning Joy - Following The Light Inside
programme.desc: 3x GRAMMY winner, J. Ivy, shares his transformation from a shy kid to a spoken word powerhouse. J. Ivy performs. Plus, a story about a bus driver who made a first grader's day, street interviews from NYC, and Daaji discusses the soul's evolution.

programme.title: Rooted In Wellness - Dr. Casey Means: Finding Optimal Metabolic Health through Ancient Wisdom
programme.desc: Did you know that your metabolic health and energy are controlled by our modern food? This means your metabolism is not broken! You just need to work on healing your metabolism through simple lifestyle shifts you can easily apply to your life today.How? This is what we dive into in this episode of Rooted in Wellness with Casey Means.

programme.title: Rooted In Wellness - Wade Lightheart + Matt Gallant: Plant-Based Muscle Building, Digestion Optimization + Balancing Urban Life
programme.desc: Do you feel both captivated and exhausted by city life? Are the conveniences worth the chaos? In this episode of the Rooted in Wellness podcast, join me, Wade Lightheart, and Matt Gallant as we explore the challenges of maintaining balance in busy urban environments and the serene contrast of ashram life.

programme.title: Rooted In Wellness - Aggie Lal: Secrets to Thriving Through Biohacking and Ancient Wisdom for Women
programme.desc: Have you wondered how to biohack your health as a woman? Taking control to live your best life is what we dive into in this episode with guest Aggie Lal.

programme.title: The Inspiration Show - How To Honor Your Freedom of Choice - Jennifer Powers
programme.desc: Jennifer shares that no matter what you experience, you get to decide how you react to these situations and what role you play in them.

programme.title: The Keeper of the Keys
programme.desc: Michael Walden is having one bad day after another and there is no end in sight. After losing his job and finding his house in foreclosure, he becomes increasingly negative and frustrated. It doesn't help that his fiancee, Anne breaks off their engagement. Depressed and discouraged, he falls asleep. Success coach Jack Canfield appears and tells Michael he is sending some friends, experts who will help him to discover 7 Keys and how to use them. Join Michael and his mysterious guide Elizabeth as they encounter a diverse group of experts eager to enlighten him.

programme.title: Relationships with Marianne Williamson - Relationships: Relationships Are Opportunities for Growth
programme.desc: The focus of today's lecture is this: atonement. While we decided to act a certain way in the past based on our personality, we can now use this information to improve our current and future relationships.

programme.title: The Secrets of the Keys
programme.desc: Elizabeth comes face-to-face with her spiritual guide, Gwen who has an intriguing opportunity for her.

programme.title: Make Believe
programme.desc: Six of the world's best young magicians battle for the title of Teen World Champion.

programme.title: Wildlike
programme.desc: Fourteen-year-old Mackenzie, a troubled teen, is sent by her mother to live with her uncle in Juneau, Alaska. She runs away and an unlikely friendship forms in the Alaskan wilderness, giving a runaway girl hope and sanctuary in America's last frontier.

programme.title: Sex Talk - Sex Talk - The Science of Falling in Love
programme.desc: The science of falling in love, plus putting the spark back into your sex life. Also, there are some health benefits to “going commando” but there are some things you always must keep in mind.

programme.title: Sex Talk - Sex Talk - Marijuana & Sex
programme.desc: Does Marijuana increase or decrease your libido? And is pregnancy brain a real thing? Plus is it possible to overdose on ED medications and a look at just what happens during vaginal rejuvenation procedures?

programme.title: Sex Talk - Sex Talk - Anger & Sex Don’t Mix
programme.desc: Did you know anger can negatively affect your performance in the bedroom? And despite what you may have heard oral sex isn’t always safe sex? Plus, don’t think you can’t get pregnant just because you’re still breastfeeding.

programme.title: The Aware Show - The Aware Show - Sensitive is the New Strong
programme.desc: Lisa Garr talks with best-selling author and spiritual teacher Anita Moorjani, who discusses life as an empath. Her latest book is “Sensitive is the New Strong,” which talks about the importance of having boundaries, especially as an empathic person.

programme.title: The Aware Show - The Aware Show -The Songwriting Journey
programme.desc: Lisa Garr talks with brothers Isaac and Thorald Koren who are on a mission to teach everyone to sing. They encourage us to open up to our creative gifts and believe that everyone can access their own personal note and singing voice.

programme.title: The Aware Show - The Aware Show - Never Alone Mental Health Summit
programme.desc: Lisa Garr talks with guests Victor Brick, Dr. Cassandra Vieten & Gabriella Wright. The group discusses the importance of Mental Wellness & working toward a more holistic view including proper nutrition, exercise, social support & eliminating stigma.

programme.title: Smart Life - Smart Life S1E10
programme.desc: Glaucoma with Dr. Barry Schechter, Autism Overview with Silvia Gil & Yumina Myers, Autism Siblings with Natalie and Jose Roman

programme.title: Smart Life - Smart Life S1E11
programme.desc: Different Types of Procedure for Back Pain with Dr. Jose Mena, Exercises at Home with Torre Washington

programme.title: Smart Life - Smart Life S1E12
programme.desc: What is Occupational Therapy? with Vickie Zamora, Ask the Expert Segment with Dr. Jonathan Fialkow, Online Dating with Paula Levine, PHD

programme.title: Smart Life - Smart Life S1E13
programme.desc: Flu Season-How to Stay Healthy with Dr. Yvonne Johnson, When to Go to the ER with Dr. Yvonne Johnson, Scoliosis in Teens with Dr. Stephen George of Nicklaus Children's Hospital

programme.title: Smart Life - Smart Life S1E14
programme.desc: Diabetes with Dr. Paul Jellinger, Identifying Eating Disorders with Melissa Spann

programme.title: Yoga For All - Yoga For All Day 1: Notice
programme.desc: Your Day 1 practice offers an invitation to slow down and take stock.

programme.title: Yoga For All - Day 2: Befriend
programme.desc: Your Day 2 session invites you to use the tools of the at-home yoga practice to befriend your body and your breath. Build on the art of noticing from Day 1 as we expand our vocabulary with a focus on a strong foundation. This session encourages a healthy flow of energy up and down the spine and lovingly guides you to learn how to take responsibility for your experience on the mat.

programme.title: Yoga For All - Day 3: Align
programme.desc: Your day 3 practice invites you to get to know your body through the structure of the practice and the art of aligning. We build on day 1 and 2 with a focus on action and alignment through asana and energy.

programme.title: Yoga For All - Day 4: Ease
programme.desc: Your Day 4 practice provides you the opportunity to lean in when you feel discomfort to embrace the process of finding ease. Discover layers of your home practice as we look at our patterns and continue to build a dynamic vocabulary that serves.

programme.title: A Return To Wholeness - A Return To Wholeness - The Social Roots of Suffering
programme.desc: Suffering has deep social causes. In this lesson, Dr. Maté explains how your physiology is impacted by stress and social determinants.

programme.title: A Return To Wholeness - A Return To Wholeness - Understanding Addiction
programme.desc: Addiction is neither a choice you make or an unavoidable disease. It’s an attempt to solve a problem. The conundrum is… it almost works.

programme.title: A Return To Wholeness - A Return To Wholeness - What Are Expressions of Healthy Anger?
programme.desc: Expressions of healthy anger are different from unhealthy anger. Dr. Maté articulates the difference and discusses the increased health risks when healthy anger is repressed.

programme.title: A Return To Wholeness - A Return To Wholeness - What Does Health Look Like?
programme.desc: What is the meaning of health? In this lesson, you’ll learn what health looks like and how to approach healing with compassionate inquiry and healthy disillusionment.

programme.title: Awakening Giants - New Zealand Part 1
programme.desc: Fifteen Giants arrive in Auckland to face their first challenge and compete in teams to with the winning team being free of all consequences of a flying leap into the unknown.

programme.title: Awakening Giants - New Zealand Part 2
programme.desc: The losers are revealed and one member from each team faces their greatest fear of heights.

programme.title: Awakening Giants - New Zealand Part 3
programme.desc: Giants go through professional speakers training in order to uplevel their skill prior to taking the stage at the International Technical University of Auckland for INSPIRE Mic

programme.title: Awakening Giants - New Zealand Part 4
programme.desc: Giants prepare to face the tallest waterfall to commercially raft in the entire world.

programme.title: Awakening Giants - New Zealand Part 5
programme.desc: In preparation to meet the oldest, pre-Maori tribe of indigenous elders, the Giants call forth the dolphins, then enter the most sacred realm of their entire lives.

programme.title: The Principle
programme.desc: A wise master is called upon to heal a painfully misguided cyberbully and his tragic victim

programme.title: Meditations for Manifestation - Meditations for Manifestation - Emotions
programme.desc: Cultivate a judgment free attitude as you explore the inner world of your thoughts and feelings.

programme.title: Tap Into Healing - Tap Into Healing: Releasing Overwhelm
programme.desc: When a healing crisis enters our life, it may show up as physical ailments, an overactive mind, exhaustion and a plethora of other imbalances. This can be very overwhelming — to the extent where we do not know where to start. In Day 1, we look at our health issues in body, mind, and spirit with open eyes, and start to clear out the overwhelm.

programme.title: Tap Into Healing - Tap Into Healing: Forgiveness
programme.desc: On Day 2, we dig deep into what is taking away our life force. Together we look at all the places we hold resistance, hate, anger, blame and shame towards yourself or others. In doing this, we are given a supreme opportunity to forgive. Forgiveness does not condone the challenges or the damage created, yet it is an “out-of-jail ticket” that allows you to let it go, so you can finally be free.

programme.title: Break Out of the Addiction Frequency - Section 1: The Addiction Frequency & The Power of Breath
programme.desc: Breathing is the single most important thing we do. Addiction is characterized by a sense of lack, of missing something. And when you breathe poorly, like when anxious or angry, you’re caught up in your head and aren’t breathing well and that sends a signal to your body that perpetuates the addiction frequency. Deep, long breathing is how we interrupt this pattern.

programme.title: Break Out of the Addiction Frequency - Section 2: Importance of Morning & Evening Practices
programme.desc: If you take care of the bookends of the day, the rest of the day will take care of itself. It’s such a powerful idea for people to understand that when we wake up in the morning, most people wake up with anxiety, fear, and negativity. So, we need to do something right away to set ourselves straight, to create the conditions for the best day possible, regardless of the circumstances that we're facing in the outside world.

programme.title: Break Out of the Addiction Frequency - Section 3: The 3 Minds NEW
programme.desc: Addiction is felt and experienced in the mind. The mind is a terrible master but a wonderful servant. We must understand its functions as it’s an important aspect of addiction recovery and learning how to thrive. So, the key question is this: How do you get the mind to serve the heart, rather than staying trapped in serving the mind? Only heightened awareness can end the inner war.

programme.title: Break Out of the Addiction Frequency - Break Out of the Addiction Frequency: Q&A
programme.desc: Questios and Answers for the series, Break Out of the Addiction Frequency.

programme.title: Hacking Your Healthcare - Day 2: Vital Signs
programme.desc: Today we start with a discussion of reference ranges, and how what medicine considers as “normal” may not be optimal for your health

programme.title: Yoga For All - Day 7: Prana
programme.desc: Your Day 7 practice is an invitation right to the good stuff. This fiery session is a warming sequence that guides to you create a healthy flow of energy in the body welcoming total mind and body health.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Forward Bending for Forward Thinking
programme.desc: Use what you learned in Day 4 to stretch the hamstrings and your backbody safely and effectively. Bring attention to your spine in Downward Facing Dog (Adho Mukha Svanasana) and Intense Side Stretch (Parsvottanasana), before working on Hand-to-Big-Toe Pose (Padangusthasana) with a strap. Finish in a supported backbend to counter your diligent forward folding.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Foundations of Forward Bending
programme.desc: In this class, you begin with a brief anatomy of forward folds, followed by a dive into Standing Forward Fold (Uttanasana) and Wide-Legged Forward Fold (Prasarita Padottanasana). Regardless of your flexibility, this lab is designed to help you to safely find length through the entire back chain of your body.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Foundations of Twisting
programme.desc: In this class, you'll investigate the mechanics of twists, one of Schuyler's personal challenges. Learn where you want to twist from—and where you don't. Apply the concept in active (think: core work!) and passive twists as well as standing, seated, and supine. You’ll finish class by massaging the base of the skull for a juicy release.

programme.title: The Superpowers of Your Gut - The Superpowers of Your Gut - Day 2
programme.desc: You have an intimate relationship with everything you put in your mouth. The tongue is a magical instrument for you to start to remember the power of flavor and nutrients, to slow it down and experience food again.

programme.title: Yoga For All - Day 7: Prana
programme.desc: Your Day 7 practice is an invitation right to the good stuff. This fiery session is a warming sequence that guides to you create a healthy flow of energy in the body welcoming total mind and body health.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Forward Bending for Forward Thinking
programme.desc: Use what you learned in Day 4 to stretch the hamstrings and your backbody safely and effectively. Bring attention to your spine in Downward Facing Dog (Adho Mukha Svanasana) and Intense Side Stretch (Parsvottanasana), before working on Hand-to-Big-Toe Pose (Padangusthasana) with a strap. Finish in a supported backbend to counter your diligent forward folding.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Foundations of Forward Bending
programme.desc: In this class, you begin with a brief anatomy of forward folds, followed by a dive into Standing Forward Fold (Uttanasana) and Wide-Legged Forward Fold (Prasarita Padottanasana). Regardless of your flexibility, this lab is designed to help you to safely find length through the entire back chain of your body.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Foundations of Twisting
programme.desc: In this class, you'll investigate the mechanics of twists, one of Schuyler's personal challenges. Learn where you want to twist from—and where you don't. Apply the concept in active (think: core work!) and passive twists as well as standing, seated, and supine. You’ll finish class by massaging the base of the skull for a juicy release.

programme.title: Thriving Through Midlife & Beyond - Thriving Through Midlife & Beyond - More Life Left Than You Think
programme.desc: Rather than seeing the coming decades as a process of getting inexorably older, what if you instead considered them an extension of the prime of your life? In fact, sociologists today consider midlife lasting as long as 40 years — from 35 to 75. This is a time for thriving and growing whole, not just old.

programme.title: Thriving Through Midlife & Beyond - Thriving Through Midlife & Beyond - From Midlife Crisis to Midlife Chrysalis
programme.desc: Certainly, midlife years can be challenging, but instead of seeing this time as crisis, what if you viewed it instead as a chrysalis? How can you begin the transformation toward a series of vibrant years ahead?

programme.title: The Wellness Master Class - The Wellness Master Class: Energy & Motivation
programme.desc: As we prepare for Day 1 tomorrow, celebrate yourself for setting out on the path to lasting, exuberant wellness. Come to the course with an open mind for earnest personal dialogue and you’ll lay the groundwork for better performance, better experiences, and true depth to your life.

programme.title: Commune Meditation Summit - CMS - Michael B. Beckwith
programme.desc: In this lesson, you will focus on the gift of the body temple, which is the signature for revealing life, love, peace, joy, wisdom, harmony, and abundance. When the body’s temple is healthy, you can hold more energy. More of life’s cosmic energy can flow through you.

programme.title: Commune Meditation Summit - CMS - Preethaji
programme.desc: Every one of us, at any point in time, is living in one of two states of being. We are either in a suffering state or we are living in a beautiful state. When you are in a beautiful state of connection, you enter a new realm where synchronicities unfold, where the randomly moving universe arranges itself into patterns to fulfill your heart's desires. Use this meditation practice to manifest heart-felt intentions in your life.

programme.title: The Superpowers of Your Gut - The Superpowers of Your Gut - Day 2
programme.desc: You have an intimate relationship with everything you put in your mouth. The tongue is a magical instrument for you to start to remember the power of flavor and nutrients, to slow it down and experience food again.

programme.title: The Superpowers of Your Gut - The Superpowers of Your Gut - Day 1
programme.desc: Human biology is a symphony played between the body and the greater nature around us. The great conductor of that symphony, in many ways, is the gut and microbiome. This course will take a multifaceted look at this organ system � which is our primary interface with the chemistry of the world � so you can get an understanding of how to co-create health and well-being.

programme.title: Breath, Body, Beats with Schuyler Grant - Breath, Body, Beats with Schuyler Grant - Day 1: Breathing with the Beats
programme.desc: Welcome to Day 1! Congratulations on making this powerful commitment to yourself and giving yourself the gift of flow state. Research shows that if you cultivate the flow state in your daily life, the benefits touch every part of your life—job performance, peace of mind, creativity, and your own sense of purpose. Connecting to the breath is that powerful. Remember that it’s important to have your blocks and yoga blanket or thick towel. If you find yourself empty-handed, you can always DIY your own. In this first class, you will learn key postures and proper shoulder alignment to establish a foundation for this challenge. (Upcoming classes include less talking and more flowing, but this one is important for everything that follows, so stick with it!)

programme.title: Breath, Body, Beats with Schuyler Grant - Breath, Body, Beats with Schuyler Grant - Day 2: Setting Up the Side Body
programme.desc: We walk around having a palpable experience of both our brain and our body. But we rarely attend to the breath. Yet the breath is the link between your mind and your body—the seen and the unseen. It’s the great constant of your life. Today, we work with the breath while paying attention to the side body. These crucial stretches help lengthen the abdominals, hips, and thighs, all while improving spinal flexibility and expanding the rib cage—great for facilitating deeper breathing. Ease through various standing poses and side stretches in Chair (Utkatasana) and Warrior 2 (Virabhadrasana II), finding ample opportunity to elongate the side body and invite in the breath. Savor this beginning.

programme.title: The Wellness Master Class - The Wellness Master Class: Whole Life Assessment
programme.desc: Today we’ll ask some tough questions of ourselves and check in with every area of our life. We’ll use what we find as a foundation to realign our goals, our lives, and to start to give more to the people and situations around us that need the most attention.

programme.title: The Wellness Master Class - The Wellness Master Class: Stress
programme.desc: Today’s course walks you through best practices, habits, and mindsets for dealing with stress. Examine your life and identify your stress points, using Brendon’s techniques to release your tension and get back to showing up like you want. Together we’ll prepare for the eventuality of stress by adjusting our relationship with it.

programme.title: Relationships with Marianne Williamson - Relationships: Relationships and Your Spiritual Journey
programme.desc: Today's lesson is a bit of a reality check, as we'll begin to question the current way we look at relationships, romantic or not. How we see ourselves and our relationship to God influences our view of our relationships with other people. Today we're going to examine that view from a place of love.

programme.title: Relationships with Marianne Williamson - Relationships: Overcoming the Ego Mind
programme.desc: In the following lesson, we'll discover how the ego mind influences both our way of thinking and our relationships. We'll also identify the unconscious barriers we've built that cause us to form these judgments and thoughts. That's where the problem is, and that's where the solution lies.

programme.title: Relationships with Marianne Williamson - Relationships: How to Have Miraculous Relationships
programme.desc: A miracle happens when you stop thinking with fear and start thinking with love. Your greatest power lies in your mind. To establish stronger connections, you need to go into every situation requesting to be an instrument of love. When you approach your interactions with love, you'll begin to have more miraculous relationships.

programme.title: The Nature of Reality with Deepak Chopra - The Nature of Reality: The Science of Reality
programme.desc: What is real? How do we know it's real, and how does that deeper understanding of reality help us fulfill our dreams, desires, and intentions? So let's go for it.

programme.title: The Nature of Reality with Deepak Chopra - The Nature of Reality: Fundamental Experience
programme.desc: Let's shift away from the scientific model to a different way of looking at that which we call reality.

programme.title: Deep Relaxation with Tracee Stanley - Intuition: Deep Knowing Relaxation Practice
programme.desc: Move beyond the mind and connect with a deeper part of yourself. Experiment and let go and explore your true nature to create a new way of being in the world.

programme.title: Good Mood Food with Jason Wrobel - Happy, Healthy Gut
programme.desc: For Day 1 of the course, Jason discusses how nutrients in food can directly affect your neurons and neurotransmitter function. He also shares why gut health is absolutely crucial for brain health and how to make super creamy and delicious dairy-free Coconut Yogurt.

programme.title: Relationships with Marianne Williamson - Relationships: Relationships and Your Spiritual Journey
programme.desc: Today's lesson is a bit of a reality check, as we'll begin to question the current way we look at relationships, romantic or not. How we see ourselves and our relationship to God influences our view of our relationships with other people. Today we're going to examine that view from a place of love.

programme.desc: In the following lesson, we'll discover how the ego mind influences both our way of thinking and our relationships. We'll also identify the unconscious barriers we've built that cause us to form these judgments and thoughts. That's where the problem is, and that's where the solution lies.

programme.title: Relationships with Marianne Williamson - Relationships: How to Have Miraculous Relationships
programme.desc: A miracle happens when you stop thinking with fear and start thinking with love. Your greatest power lies in your mind. To establish stronger connections, you need to go into every situation requesting to be an instrument of love. When you approach your interactions with love, you'll begin to have more miraculous relationships.

programme.title: The Nature of Reality with Deepak Chopra - The Nature of Reality: Fundamental Experience
programme.desc: Let's shift away from the scientific model to a different way of looking at that which we call reality.

programme.title: Good Mood Food with Jason Wrobel - Feed Your Neurotransmitters
programme.desc: It’s time for a little science class, but way more fun and interesting! Jason shares the basics of neuroscience: what a neurotransmitter is and how it functions, along with the top three emotional neurotransmitters that are key to your mood and overall emotional health.

programme.title: Deep Relaxation with Tracee Stanley - Intuition: Deep Knowing Relaxation Practice
programme.desc: Move beyond the mind and connect with a deeper part of yourself. Experiment and let go and explore your true nature to create a new way of being in the world.

programme.title: Good Mood Food with Jason Wrobel - Mood Disorders & Depression
programme.desc: Today, Jason’s getting vulnerable with you. He shares his personal story of how he was diagnosed with clinical depression and his journey into healing. His protocol includes supplements, new foods, meditation, and somatic therapy.

programme.title: Tap Into Healing - Tap Into Healing: Releasing Overwhelm
programme.desc: When a healing crisis enters our life, it may show up as physical ailments, an overactive mind, exhaustion and a plethora of other imbalances. This can be very overwhelming — to the extent where we do not know where to start. In Day 1, we look at our health issues in body, mind, and spirit with open eyes, and start to clear out the overwhelm.

programme.title: Good Mood Food with Jason Wrobel - Feed Your Neurotransmitters
programme.desc: It’s time for a little science class, but way more fun and interesting! Jason shares the basics of neuroscience: what a neurotransmitter is and how it functions, along with the top three emotional neurotransmitters that are key to your mood and overall emotional health.

programme.title: Tap Into Healing - Tap Into Healing: Forgiveness
programme.desc: On Day 2, we dig deep into what is taking away our life force. Together we look at all the places we hold resistance, hate, anger, blame and shame towards yourself or others. In doing this, we are given a supreme opportunity to forgive. Forgiveness does not condone the challenges or the damage created, yet it is an “out-of-jail ticket” that allows you to let it go, so you can finally be free.

programme.title: Break Out of the Addiction Frequency - Section 1: The Addiction Frequency & The Power of Breath
programme.desc: Breathing is the single most important thing we do. Addiction is characterized by a sense of lack, of missing something. And when you breathe poorly, like when anxious or angry, you’re caught up in your head and aren’t breathing well and that sends a signal to your body that perpetuates the addiction frequency. Deep, long breathing is how we interrupt this pattern.

programme.title: Break Out of the Addiction Frequency - Section 2: Importance of Morning & Evening Practices
programme.desc: If you take care of the bookends of the day, the rest of the day will take care of itself. It’s such a powerful idea for people to understand that when we wake up in the morning, most people wake up with anxiety, fear, and negativity. So, we need to do something right away to set ourselves straight, to create the conditions for the best day possible, regardless of the circumstances that we're facing in the outside world.

programme.title: Break Out of the Addiction Frequency - Section 3: The 3 Minds NEW
programme.desc: Addiction is felt and experienced in the mind. The mind is a terrible master but a wonderful servant. We must understand its functions as it’s an important aspect of addiction recovery and learning how to thrive. So, the key question is this: How do you get the mind to serve the heart, rather than staying trapped in serving the mind? Only heightened awareness can end the inner war.

programme.title: Break Out of the Addiction Frequency - Break Out of the Addiction Frequency: Q&A
programme.desc: Questios and Answers for the series, Break Out of the Addiction Frequency.

programme.title: Hacking Your Healthcare - Day 2: Vital Signs
programme.desc: Today we start with a discussion of reference ranges, and how what medicine considers as “normal” may not be optimal for your health

programme.title: Yoga For All - Day 7: Prana
programme.desc: Your Day 7 practice is an invitation right to the good stuff. This fiery session is a warming sequence that guides to you create a healthy flow of energy in the body welcoming total mind and body health.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Forward Bending for Forward Thinking
programme.desc: Use what you learned in Day 4 to stretch the hamstrings and your backbody safely and effectively. Bring attention to your spine in Downward Facing Dog (Adho Mukha Svanasana) and Intense Side Stretch (Parsvottanasana), before working on Hand-to-Big-Toe Pose (Padangusthasana) with a strap. Finish in a supported backbend to counter your diligent forward folding.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Foundations of Forward Bending
programme.desc: In this class, you begin with a brief anatomy of forward folds, followed by a dive into Standing Forward Fold (Uttanasana) and Wide-Legged Forward Fold (Prasarita Padottanasana). Regardless of your flexibility, this lab is designed to help you to safely find length through the entire back chain of your body.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Foundations of Twisting
programme.desc: In this class, you'll investigate the mechanics of twists, one of Schuyler's personal challenges. Learn where you want to twist from—and where you don't. Apply the concept in active (think: core work!) and passive twists as well as standing, seated, and supine. You’ll finish class by massaging the base of the skull for a juicy release.

programme.title: The Superpowers of Your Gut - The Superpowers of Your Gut - Day 2
programme.desc: You have an intimate relationship with everything you put in your mouth. The tongue is a magical instrument for you to start to remember the power of flavor and nutrients, to slow it down and experience food again.

programme.title: Yoga For All - Day 7: Prana
programme.desc: Your Day 7 practice is an invitation right to the good stuff. This fiery session is a warming sequence that guides to you create a healthy flow of energy in the body welcoming total mind and body health.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Forward Bending for Forward Thinking
programme.desc: Use what you learned in Day 4 to stretch the hamstrings and your backbody safely and effectively. Bring attention to your spine in Downward Facing Dog (Adho Mukha Svanasana) and Intense Side Stretch (Parsvottanasana), before working on Hand-to-Big-Toe Pose (Padangusthasana) with a strap. Finish in a supported backbend to counter your diligent forward folding.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Foundations of Forward Bending
programme.desc: In this class, you begin with a brief anatomy of forward folds, followed by a dive into Standing Forward Fold (Uttanasana) and Wide-Legged Forward Fold (Prasarita Padottanasana). Regardless of your flexibility, this lab is designed to help you to safely find length through the entire back chain of your body.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Foundations of Twisting
programme.desc: In this class, you'll investigate the mechanics of twists, one of Schuyler's personal challenges. Learn where you want to twist from—and where you don't. Apply the concept in active (think: core work!) and passive twists as well as standing, seated, and supine. You’ll finish class by massaging the base of the skull for a juicy release.

programme.title: Thriving Through Midlife & Beyond - Thriving Through Midlife & Beyond - More Life Left Than You Think
programme.desc: Rather than seeing the coming decades as a process of getting inexorably older, what if you instead considered them an extension of the prime of your life? In fact, sociologists today consider midlife lasting as long as 40 years — from 35 to 75. This is a time for thriving and growing whole, not just old.

programme.title: Thriving Through Midlife & Beyond - Thriving Through Midlife & Beyond - From Midlife Crisis to Midlife Chrysalis
programme.desc: Certainly, midlife years can be challenging, but instead of seeing this time as crisis, what if you viewed it instead as a chrysalis? How can you begin the transformation toward a series of vibrant years ahead?

programme.title: The Wellness Master Class - The Wellness Master Class: Energy & Motivation
programme.desc: As we prepare for Day 1 tomorrow, celebrate yourself for setting out on the path to lasting, exuberant wellness. Come to the course with an open mind for earnest personal dialogue and you’ll lay the groundwork for better performance, better experiences, and true depth to your life.

programme.title: Commune Meditation Summit - CMS - Michael B. Beckwith
programme.desc: In this lesson, you will focus on the gift of the body temple, which is the signature for revealing life, love, peace, joy, wisdom, harmony, and abundance. When the body’s temple is healthy, you can hold more energy. More of life’s cosmic energy can flow through you.

programme.title: Commune Meditation Summit - CMS - Preethaji
programme.desc: Every one of us, at any point in time, is living in one of two states of being. We are either in a suffering state or we are living in a beautiful state. When you are in a beautiful state of connection, you enter a new realm where synchronicities unfold, where the randomly moving universe arranges itself into patterns to fulfill your heart's desires. Use this meditation practice to manifest heart-felt intentions in your life.

programme.title: The Superpowers of Your Gut - The Superpowers of Your Gut - Day 2
programme.desc: You have an intimate relationship with everything you put in your mouth. The tongue is a magical instrument for you to start to remember the power of flavor and nutrients, to slow it down and experience food again.

programme.title: The Superpowers of Your Gut - The Superpowers of Your Gut - Day 1
programme.desc: Human biology is a symphony played between the body and the greater nature around us. The great conductor of that symphony, in many ways, is the gut and microbiome. This course will take a multifaceted look at this organ system � which is our primary interface with the chemistry of the world � so you can get an understanding of how to co-create health and well-being.

programme.title: Breath, Body, Beats with Schuyler Grant - Breath, Body, Beats with Schuyler Grant - Day 1: Breathing with the Beats
programme.desc: Welcome to Day 1! Congratulations on making this powerful commitment to yourself and giving yourself the gift of flow state. Research shows that if you cultivate the flow state in your daily life, the benefits touch every part of your life—job performance, peace of mind, creativity, and your own sense of purpose. Connecting to the breath is that powerful. Remember that it’s important to have your blocks and yoga blanket or thick towel. If you find yourself empty-handed, you can always DIY your own. In this first class, you will learn key postures and proper shoulder alignment to establish a foundation for this challenge. (Upcoming classes include less talking and more flowing, but this one is important for everything that follows, so stick with it!)

programme.title: Breath, Body, Beats with Schuyler Grant - Breath, Body, Beats with Schuyler Grant - Day 2: Setting Up the Side Body
programme.desc: We walk around having a palpable experience of both our brain and our body. But we rarely attend to the breath. Yet the breath is the link between your mind and your body—the seen and the unseen. It’s the great constant of your life. Today, we work with the breath while paying attention to the side body. These crucial stretches help lengthen the abdominals, hips, and thighs, all while improving spinal flexibility and expanding the rib cage—great for facilitating deeper breathing. Ease through various standing poses and side stretches in Chair (Utkatasana) and Warrior 2 (Virabhadrasana II), finding ample opportunity to elongate the side body and invite in the breath. Savor this beginning.

programme.title: The Wellness Master Class - The Wellness Master Class: Whole Life Assessment
programme.desc: Today we’ll ask some tough questions of ourselves and check in with every area of our life. We’ll use what we find as a foundation to realign our goals, our lives, and to start to give more to the people and situations around us that need the most attention.

programme.title: The Wellness Master Class - The Wellness Master Class: Stress
programme.desc: Today’s course walks you through best practices, habits, and mindsets for dealing with stress. Examine your life and identify your stress points, using Brendon’s techniques to release your tension and get back to showing up like you want. Together we’ll prepare for the eventuality of stress by adjusting our relationship with it.

programme.title: Relationships with Marianne Williamson - Relationships: Relationships and Your Spiritual Journey
programme.desc: Today's lesson is a bit of a reality check, as we'll begin to question the current way we look at relationships, romantic or not. How we see ourselves and our relationship to God influences our view of our relationships with other people. Today we're going to examine that view from a place of love.

programme.title: Relationships with Marianne Williamson - Relationships: Overcoming the Ego Mind
programme.desc: In the following lesson, we'll discover how the ego mind influences both our way of thinking and our relationships. We'll also identify the unconscious barriers we've built that cause us to form these judgments and thoughts. That's where the problem is, and that's where the solution lies.

programme.title: Relationships with Marianne Williamson - Relationships: How to Have Miraculous Relationships
programme.desc: A miracle happens when you stop thinking with fear and start thinking with love. Your greatest power lies in your mind. To establish stronger connections, you need to go into every situation requesting to be an instrument of love. When you approach your interactions with love, you'll begin to have more miraculous relationships.

programme.title: The Nature of Reality with Deepak Chopra - The Nature of Reality: The Science of Reality
programme.desc: What is real? How do we know it's real, and how does that deeper understanding of reality help us fulfill our dreams, desires, and intentions? So let's go for it.

programme.title: The Nature of Reality with Deepak Chopra - The Nature of Reality: Fundamental Experience
programme.desc: Let's shift away from the scientific model to a different way of looking at that which we call reality.

programme.title: Deep Relaxation with Tracee Stanley - Intuition: Deep Knowing Relaxation Practice
programme.desc: Move beyond the mind and connect with a deeper part of yourself. Experiment and let go and explore your true nature to create a new way of being in the world.

programme.title: Good Mood Food with Jason Wrobel - Happy, Healthy Gut
programme.desc: For Day 1 of the course, Jason discusses how nutrients in food can directly affect your neurons and neurotransmitter function. He also shares why gut health is absolutely crucial for brain health and how to make super creamy and delicious dairy-free Coconut Yogurt.

programme.title: Relationships with Marianne Williamson - Relationships: Relationships and Your Spiritual Journey
programme.desc: Today's lesson is a bit of a reality check, as we'll begin to question the current way we look at relationships, romantic or not. How we see ourselves and our relationship to God influences our view of our relationships with other people. Today we're going to examine that view from a place of love.

To establish stronger connections, you need to go into every situation requesting to be an instrument of love. When you approach your interactions with love, you'll begin to have more miraculous relationships.

programme.title: Good Morning Joy - Good Morning Joy - Practical Spirituality with Dr. Joe Vitale
programme.desc: Shari chats with Dr. Joe Vitale, bestselling author and spiritual teacher, about practical spirituality, conquering inner saboteurs, and his book Zero Limits Living. Plus, a joyful face-off challenge!

programme.title: Good Morning Joy - Good Morning Joy - How to Be Brave with Laura Di Franco
programme.desc: Laura di Franco, founder of Brave Healer Productions, shares her vision for Brave Healer and the power of authentic messages. Plus, authors Angel Rohrer and Carolyne McGee discuss bravery, spirituality, and their books.

programme.title: HEAL
programme.desc: Director Kelly Noonan Gores’ HEAL takes us on a scientific and spiritual journey where we discover that our thoughts, beliefs, and emotions have a huge impact on our health and ability to heal. We have more control over our health and life than we have been taught to believe.

programme.title: Break Out of the Addiction Frequency - Section 1: The Addiction Frequency & The Power of Breath
programme.desc: Breathing is the single most important thing we do. Addiction is characterized by a sense of lack, of missing something. And when you breathe poorly, like when anxious or angry, you’re caught up in your head and aren’t breathing well and that sends a signal to your body that perpetuates the addiction frequency. programme.title: Break Out of the Addiction Frequency - Break Out of the Addiction Frequency: Q&A
programme.desc: Questios and Answers for the series, Break Out of the Addiction Frequency.

programme.title: A Return To Wholeness - A Return To Wholeness - Human Nature vs. Modern Culture
programme.desc: As humans, we need interconnection: We rely on others to survive, especially as children. Yet, we also strive for authenticity. This creates an inherent tension. In this lesson, Dr. Maté discusses what is true human nature — and how the modern world has warped our understanding of our needs.

programme.title: A Return To Wholeness - A Return To Wholeness - Approaching Trauma with Curiosity and Compassion
programme.desc: Trauma is a wound that you sustain; it's not what happened to you. In this lesson, Dr. Maté explains why traumatic wounds get triggered and how to understand triggers in your process of transformation and healing.

programme.title: The Superpowers of Your Gut - The Superpowers of Your Gut - Day 8
programme.desc: We need to nurture this complex micronutrient and quantum physical exchange that is happening in the colon wall, and, in an amazing way, the nose is the perfect place to start.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: This is Your Spine on Yoga
programme.desc: Apply everything you've learned so far in this well-rounded sequence of twists, folds, and heart openers. Some points to remember: Lengthen the spine out of the pelvis before back bending, focus your twists toward the thoracic spine (the middle twelve vertebrae—not your neck or lower back), and discover the essence of a back bend in every forward fold.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Shoulder & Wrist Mobility
programme.desc: Did you know you can floss your shoulder blades? You’ll earn the fundamentals of good shoulder health—including what's your scapula and what it does—in this arm-focused class. You’ll begin by warming the shoulders and opening the front of the chest, followed by exercises to strengthen and align the wrists.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Front Shoulders Feelin' Fine
programme.desc: Explore your newfound spaciousness in this shoulder lovin' class. Waste no time waking up the wrists, then move through variations of Low Lunge (Anjaneyasana) with Cactus Arms before building to Side Plank (Vasisthasana). Counter those actions with Eagle Arms for a deep stretch across the upper back and some seated twisting.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Gliding Into the Overhead Plane
programme.desc: Get the arms up and those shoulder blades sliding! Start with a shoulder warmup as Schuyler teaches proper alignment when lifting the arms overhead. Then get into side body strengthening and visit Downward Facing Dog (Adho Mukha Svanasana). Finish with some handstand playtime at the wall and a savory chest opener.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Arms in the Air Like You Care!
programme.desc: Upward reaching provides a great opportunity for opening the side body. Begin on your feet with gentle side stretches and bound forward folds. Then build heat in earnest with Chair Pose (Utkatasana), a variety of lunges, Standing Split, and Locust (Shalabasana), followed by handstand hops. This shoulder flow is a full-body experience!

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Strong Triceps, Open Armpits
programme.desc: Strengthen and open the triceps to deepen your understanding of the upper arms. Your challenge poses for this class are Forearm Plank, Dolphin Pose, and Forearm Stand (Pincha Mayurasana)—and of course Child's Pose is always an option! Creative propwork helps you access these asanas in new and interesting ways.

programme.title: Vinyasa for Life - Vinyasa for Life: Forearms Are Forever
programme.desc: Ready for your forearms to be your best friends? This flow plays with dropped prayer variations, Cow Face Pose (Gomukhasana) arms, and, of course, Forearm Plank and Side Plank. Expect some back-bending Pigeons and flowing Cobras in there as well. End on your back with a solid Bridge Pose (Setu Bandhasana) to close out this sweet and intense practice.

programme.title: Breath, Body, Beats with Schuyler Grant - Breath, Body, Beats with Schuyler Grant - Day 1: Breathing with the Beats
programme.desc: Welcome to Day 1! Congratulations on making this powerful commitment to yourself and giving yourself the gift of flow state. Research shows that if you cultivate the flow state in your daily life, the benefits touch every part of your life—job performance, peace of mind, creativity, and your own sense of purpose. Connecting to the breath is that powerful. Remember that it’s important to have your blocks and yoga blanket or thick towel. If you find yourself empty-handed, you can always DIY your own. In this first class, you will learn key postures and proper shoulder alignment to establish a foundation for this challenge. (Upcoming classes include less talking and more flowing, but this one is important for everything that follows, so stick with it!)

programme.title: HEAL
programme.desc: Director Kelly Noonan Gores’ HEAL takes us on a scientific and spiritual journey where we discover that our thoughts, beliefs, and emotions have a huge impact on our health and ability to heal. We have more control over our health and life than we have been taught to believe.

programme.title: The Highest Pass
programme.desc: Part heart-pounding adventure, part spiritual quest, THE HIGHEST PASS, a feature length documentary, premiered in Los Angeles to sold out audiences. The award-winning film shares the journey of seven seekers, including one who has never ridden a motorcycle, who follow a guru with a Vedic death prophecy on a moto journey up to the highest pass of the Indian Himalayas. Over 21 days and up to an elevation of 18,000 feet along a winding road, they never let low oxygen, altitude sickness, river-flooded roads, icy edges, snow and loaded trucks dampen their spirits or, ultimately, their deeper selves.

programme.title: Fantastic Fungi: Remastered
programme.desc: Fantastic Fungi Remastered is a breathtaking journey into the hidden world of mycelium. Through stunning imagery and expert insights, the film reveals fungi’s beauty, intelligence, and power to heal, inspire, and offer solutions for our planet’s future.

programme.title: Rooted In Wellness - 1. 5 Ways to Become Rooted in Wellness
programme.desc: In our very first solo episode of Rooted in Wellness, we explore the transformative power of being anchored like a sturdy tree—steady, grounded, and unwavering amidst life's storms. We'll illuminate how feeling rooted manifests as inner peace, emotional stability, and a profound connection with yourself and the world around you.

programme.title: Rooted In Wellness - Dr. Mindy Pelz: How to Become Hormonally Literate through Ancient Healing Practices
programme.desc: Have you ever felt like your hormones are playing a tune out of sync with your well-being? Chances are, they probably are. The good news? There's a transformative journey waiting for women when they realign with nature, pay attention to their meals, and practice intermittent fasting the right way.

programme.title: Rooted In Wellness - Dr. Casey Means: Finding Optimal Metabolic Health through Ancient Wisdom
programme.desc: Did you know that your metabolic health and energy are controlled by our modern food? This means your metabolism is not broken! You just need to work on healing your metabolism through simple lifestyle shifts you can easily apply to your life today.How? This is what we dive into in this episode of Rooted in Wellness with Casey Means.

programme.title: Sex Talk - Sex Talk - The Sexual Benefits of Working Out
programme.desc: Did you know being active in the gym could improve your activity in the bedroom? Everything you wanted to ask about sex but didn’t. Now you can on Sex Talk with Dr. Lia.

programme.title: Sex Talk - Sex Talk - It’s Alright to Talk About Sex
programme.desc: A man gets terrible headaches right at the height of passion. And a woman won’t talk about her sexual desires. What should her partner do? All this and more on Sex Talk with Dr. Lia.

programme.title: Sex Talk - Sex Talk - Is There Really a G Spot?
programme.desc: A man wonders if he should look for love while his wife battles dementia and the classic tale of the G spot gets a rewriting. This week on Sex Talk with Dr. Lia.

programme.title: Rooted In Wellness - 1. 5 Ways to Become Rooted in Wellness
programme.desc: In our very first solo episode of Rooted in Wellness, we explore the transformative power of being anchored like a sturdy tree—steady, grounded, and unwavering amidst life's storms.
